tools.name: webhooks_create
tools.description:
Create a new webhook to receive real-time notifications from Pipedrive.

Creates a webhook that will send HTTP POST requests to your endpoint when specified events occur.

Required fields:
- subscription_url: The HTTPS endpoint that will receive webhook notifications
- event_action: The action that triggers the webhook
- event_object: The object type that triggers the webhook

Webhook triggers (event_action + event_object):
- Event Actions: added, updated, deleted, merged, * (all)
- Event Objects: activity, activityType, deal, note, organization, person, pipeline, product, stage, user, * (all)

Common webhook combinations:
- New deals: { "event_action": "added", "event_object": "deal" }
- Updated persons: { "event_action": "updated", "event_object": "person" }
- Deleted organizations: { "event_action": "deleted", "event_object": "organization" }
- All events: { "event_action": "*", "event_object": "*" }
- All deal events: { "event_action": "*", "event_object": "deal" }

Security:
- Use HTTPS URLs for security
- Optionally add HTTP Basic Auth with http_auth_user and http_auth_password
- Both auth fields must be provided together

Version:
- version: "2.0" (recommended, default) or "1.0" (legacy)
- v2.0 includes more data and better structure

Workflow tips:
1. Set up your endpoint first to handle POST requests
2. Create the webhook with appropriate event filters
3. Test with webhooks/list to verify creation
4. Monitor last_delivery_time and last_http_status for debugging
5. Your endpoint should return 200-299 status code to acknowledge receipt

Example payloads:
- Simple webhook: { "subscription_url": "https://example.com/webhook", "event_action": "added", "event_object": "deal" }
- With auth: { "subscription_url": "https://example.com/webhook", "event_action": "updated", "event_object": "person", "http_auth_user": "user", "http_auth_password": "pass" }
- All events: { "subscription_url": "https://example.com/webhook", "event_action": "*", "event_object": "*", "name": "All Events Webhook" }

Important notes:
- Pipedrive will send a test request immediately after creation
- Your endpoint must be accessible from Pipedrive servers
- Webhooks that repeatedly fail may be automatically disabled
- See Pipedrive documentation for webhook payload structure

tools.name: webhooks_delete
tools.description:
Delete a webhook by its ID.

Permanently removes a webhook from Pipedrive. Once deleted, the webhook will no longer send notifications to the subscription URL.

When to delete webhooks:
- The endpoint URL is no longer valid or in use
- You want to stop receiving notifications for specific events
- You're cleaning up test/development webhooks
- The integration using the webhook has been decommissioned
- The webhook is failing consistently and needs to be recreated

Workflow:
1. Use webhooks/list to get the ID of the webhook you want to delete
2. Call webhooks/delete with the webhook ID
3. Confirm deletion was successful

Important notes:
- This action is permanent and cannot be undone
- Deleting a webhook will immediately stop all notifications
- You can recreate a webhook with the same settings later if needed
- Use webhooks/list after deletion to verify it was removed

Example:
- Delete webhook: { "id": 123 }

Best practices:
- Always verify the webhook ID before deletion
- Document which webhooks are deleted and why
- Consider disabling temporarily instead if you might need it again
- Keep track of webhook configurations for recreating if needed

tools.name: webhooks_list
tools.description:
Get all webhooks configured for the company.

Returns a list of all webhooks that have been created in Pipedrive, including:
- Active and inactive webhooks
- Event triggers (action + object combinations)
- Subscription URLs
- Authentication settings
- Last delivery information

Note: Webhook data is always fetched fresh (no caching) to ensure you see the current state.

Common use cases:
- List all active webhooks: Review current webhook configurations
- Audit webhook endpoints: Check which URLs are receiving notifications
- Debug webhook issues: View last delivery time and HTTP status
- Manage webhooks: Get webhook IDs before updating or deleting

Event combinations explained:
- event_action: added, updated, deleted, merged, or * (all)
- event_object: activity, deal, person, organization, note, pipeline, product, stage, user, or * (all)
- Examples: "added.deal", "updated.person", "deleted.organization", "*.*" (all events)

Webhook fields returned:
- id: Webhook ID
- subscription_url: The URL that receives webhook notifications
- event_action & event_object: The trigger combination
- is_active: Whether the webhook is currently active
- add_time: When the webhook was created
- last_delivery_time: Last successful delivery timestamp
- last_http_status: HTTP status code from last delivery attempt
- version: Webhook API version (1.0 or 2.0)
- name: Optional webhook name

connectors.name: Gong
connectors.description: Sales call intelligence: list calls, get transcripts and summaries, search recordings, list users. 'Show my calls from last week' or 'Get the transcript from the Acme demo call'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. Click 'Open Gong' below to go to API settings
2. Click 'Get API credentials' (requires Technical Administrator role)
3. Copy the Access Key and Access Key Secret
4. Paste both values here

Note: Requires Gong Enterprise plan with API access enabled. Contact your Gong admin if API settings are not visible.

tools.name: get_call_summary
tools.description: Get an AI-generated summary of a single call including brief overview, key points, topics, action items, and detailed outline. This is the recommended way to understand a call - use get_call_transcript only if you need exact quotes.

tools.name: get_call_transcript
tools.description: Get the raw transcript for a single call with speaker-attributed text. Only use this when you need exact quotes - prefer get_call_summary for understanding call content. Transcripts are truncated by default (10KB) to prevent context overflow - use maxLength and offset to paginate.

tools.name: list_calls
tools.description: List Gong calls with optional date filtering. Returns minimal call metadata (ID, title, date, duration). Use get_call_summary for details or get_call_transcript for full transcript.

tools.name: list_users
tools.description: List all Gong users in your workspace. Returns user details including name, email, and title.

tools.name: search_calls
tools.description: Search for Gong calls with advanced filters including date range, workspace, primary users (call hosts), and specific call IDs. More flexible than list_calls for targeted queries.

connectors.name: Shopify
connectors.description: Shopify store management: products, collections, customers, orders, draft orders, inventory, metafields. Search, create, update across your store.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. Click 'Open Shopify' below to go to your Shopify admin
2. Go to Settings → Apps and sales channels → Develop apps
3. Click 'Create an app' and name it (e.g., 'Rebel AI')
4. Click 'Configure Admin API scopes' and enable:
   read_products, write_products, read_customers, write_customers,
   read_orders, write_orders, read_draft_orders, write_draft_orders,
   read_inventory, write_inventory, read_locations
5. Click 'Install app' and confirm
6. Copy the Admin API access token — you won't see it again!
7. Paste your store domain and access token below

Note: Requires a Shopify store with custom app development enabled. Node.js required.

connectors.name: Gamma
connectors.description: Gamma AI presentations, documents, and websites. Describe what you want: topic, audience, tone, length. Returns a link to your generated content. 'Create a 10-slide pitch deck about our Q4 results for investors' or 'Make a one-pager explaining our product for new customers'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. Click 'Open Gamma' below to go to API Keys page
2. Click 'Create API Key'
3. Name your key (e.g., 'Rebel AI')
4. Copy the API key - you won't see it again!
5. Paste it here

tools.name: configure_gamma_api_key
tools.description:
Configure the Gamma API key for this session.

WHEN TO USE: Only call this if you get an error saying "Gamma API key not configured"

HOW USER GETS THEIR KEY:
1. Go to https://gamma.app/settings/developers
2. Click "Create API Key"
3. Copy the key (starts with "sk-gamma-")

After configuring, all other gamma_* tools will work immediately.

tools.name: gamma_create_from_template
tools.description:
Clone and modify an existing Gamma presentation/document using AI.

USE CASE: User has an existing gamma they want to repurpose with different content while keeping the structure/design.

HOW TO GET THE GAMMA ID:
- From URL like "https://gamma.app/docs/My-Deck-abc123xyz" -> gamma_id is "abc123xyz"
- It's the last part of the gamma URL after the title

ASYNC WORKFLOW (same as gamma_generate):
1. Call gamma_create_from_template -> returns generation_id
2. Poll gamma_get_status every 3-5 seconds until "completed"

EXAMPLE:
{ "gamma_id": "abc123xyz", "prompt": "Update this pitch deck for our Series B raise, focusing on our 2025 metrics" }

tools.name: gamma_generate
tools.description:
Create AI-powered presentations, documents, webpages, or social posts with Gamma.

IMPORTANT - ASYNC WORKFLOW (you MUST follow these steps):
1. Call gamma_generate -> returns a generation_id immediately
2. Call gamma_get_status(generation_id) repeatedly every 3-5 seconds
3. Continue polling until status is "completed" or "failed"
4. When completed, you'll get gamma_url (and pdf_url/pptx_url if export_as was set)

APPLYING THEMES (corporate branding):
1. First call gamma_list_themes to see available themes
2. Look for type:"custom" themes - these are workspace/corporate themes
3. Pass the theme's "id" field to theme_id parameter

QUICK START EXAMPLES:
- Simple: { "input_text": "AI trends in 2025" }
- With theme: { "input_text": "Q4 Sales Review", "theme_id": "abc123def" }
- Professional deck: { "input_text": "Product launch plan", "text_tone": "professional", "text_audience": "executives", "num_cards": 12 }
- Export to PDF: { "input_text": "Training manual", "format": "document", "export_as": "pdf" }

DO NOT assume the generation is complete immediately - you MUST poll gamma_get_status.

tools.name: gamma_get_status
tools.description:
Poll the status of a Gamma generation. REQUIRED after calling gamma_generate or gamma_create_from_template.

When an export format was requested during generation, this tool automatically waits up to 60 seconds for the export URL to become available.

RESPONSE STATUS VALUES:
- "pending": Still generating. Call again in 3-5 seconds.
- "completed": Done! Response includes gamma_url. Also pdf_url/pptx_url if export_as was set.
- "failed": Generation failed. Check the error field for details.

TYPICAL USAGE PATTERN:
1. Get generation_id from gamma_generate response
2. Loop: call gamma_get_status, check status, sleep 3-5 seconds if pending
3. Return the gamma_url to user when completed

COMPLETED RESPONSE EXAMPLE:
{
  "status": "completed",
  "gamma_url": "https://gamma.app/docs/My-Presentation-xyz123",
  "pdf_url": "https://...",  // only if export_as:"pdf" was set
  "file_path": "/tmp/gamma_export_abc123_1709366400000.pdf",  // local download, use this instead of the URL
  "credits": { "deducted": 150, "remaining": 2850 }
}

tools.name: gamma_list_folders
tools.description:
List folders in the user's Gamma workspace for organizing presentations.

USE CASE: User wants to save a gamma to a specific folder (e.g., "Client Decks", "Internal")

HOW TO USE:
1. Call gamma_list_folders to see available folders
2. Pass folder "id" to gamma_generate's folder_ids parameter (array of strings)

PAGINATION: If has_more is true, pass next_cursor as the "after" parameter to get more results.

RESPONSE EXAMPLE:
{
  "folders": [
    { "id": "folder_abc123", "name": "Client Presentations" },
    { "id": "folder_xyz789", "name": "Internal Docs" }
  ],
  "has_more": false
}

tools.name: gamma_list_themes
tools.description:
List available Gamma themes. Call this FIRST when user wants to apply corporate/custom branding.

THEME TYPES IN RESPONSE:
- type: "standard" = Global themes available to everyone (e.g., "Oasis", "Prism")
- type: "custom" = Workspace-specific themes (corporate branding, company themes)

HOW TO USE FOR CORPORATE THEMES:
1. Call gamma_list_themes (no parameters needed)
2. Look for themes where type is "custom" - these are the user's corporate themes
3. Use the "id" field when calling gamma_generate with theme_id parameter

PAGINATION: If has_more is true, pass next_cursor as the "after" parameter to get more results.

RESPONSE EXAMPLE:
{
  "themes": [
    { "id": "abc123", "name": "Mindstone Brand", "type": "custom", "colorKeywords": ["blue", "professional"] },
    { "id": "xyz789", "name": "Oasis", "type": "standard", "colorKeywords": ["green", "nature"] }
  ],
  "has_more": true,
  "next_cursor": "eyJpZCI6Inh5ejc4OSJ9"
}

connectors.name: Napkin AI
connectors.description: Napkin AI visual generation. Turn text into professional diagrams, infographics, and illustrations — mindmaps, timelines, flowcharts, comparisons, and more. 'Create a visual diagram of our product architecture' or 'Turn these bullet points into an infographic'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. Click 'Open Napkin AI' below to go to Napkin
2. Go to Account Settings (or Team Space Settings)
3. Open the Developers tab
4. Create a new API token
5. Copy and paste the token here

tools.name: configure_napkin_api_key
tools.description:
Configure the Napkin AI API key for this session.

WHEN TO USE: Only call this if you get an error saying "Napkin API key not configured".

HOW USER GETS THEIR KEY:
1. Go to https://app.napkin.ai → Account Settings (or Team Space Settings)
2. Open the Developers tab
3. Create a new API token

After configuring, all other napkin_* tools will work immediately.

tools.name: napkin_check_status
tools.description:
Check the status of a Napkin visual generation request. REQUIRED after calling napkin_generate_visual.

RESPONSE STATUS VALUES:
- "pending": Still generating. Call again in 3-5 seconds.
- "completed": Done! Response includes generated_files with download URLs, dimensions, and style info.
- "failed": Generation failed. Check the error field for details (e.g., no_credits, no_visuals).

IMPORTANT: File download URLs expire after 30 minutes. Download promptly or use napkin_download_visual to save to disk.

COMPLETED RESPONSE EXAMPLE:
{
  "request_id": "...",
  "status": "completed",
  "generated_files": [
    { "url": "https://...", "visual_id": "...", "style_id": "...", "width": 570, "height": 630 }
  ],
  "credits": { "consumed": 20 }
}

WARNINGS: The response may include warnings about partial failures:
- missing_visual_ids: Some requested layouts didn't fit the content
- invalid_style_id: Style not found, default was used instead
- not_enough_visuals: Fewer visuals generated than requested

tools.name: napkin_download_visual
tools.description:
Download a generated Napkin visual file to disk.

Use this after napkin_check_status returns "completed" to save files locally.
Pass a file URL from the generated_files array in the status response.

Files are saved to your private space (Chief-of-Staff/generated-visuals/) in the workspace, or ~/Pictures/NapkinVisuals/ if no workspace is set.

IMPORTANT: Download URLs expire 30 minutes after generation. Call this promptly after getting a completed status.

EXAMPLE:
{ "file_url": "https://api.napkin.ai/v1/visual/.../file/...", "filename": "product-architecture" }

tools.name: napkin_generate_visual

connectors.name: ProfitSage
connectors.description: ProfitSage hospitality BI — implemented against the ProfitSword Data Portal v3 API spec, so any ProfitSage tenant with Data Portal v3 enabled can connect. List hotel sites, pull daily/monthly P&L, labor, GL ledger batches, sales bookings and sales pace (events, rooms, transient). 'What was RevPAR for Embassy Suites College Station last month?' or 'Which properties missed labor budget this week?'
connectors.setupInstructions:
Rebel connects to ProfitSage via the ProfitSword Data Portal v3 API — the same spec every ProfitSage tenant uses. Enter your tenant details:

1. Subdomain: the part before '.profitsage.net' in your ProfitSage URL (e.g. if your URL is 'acmehotels.profitsage.net', your subdomain is 'acmehotels')
2. API username: ask your ProfitSage administrator for an API service account — do not use a personal login
3. API password: paired with the API username

Your tenant must have Data Portal v3 enabled; if you're not sure, ask your ProfitSage administrator. ProfitSage issues a 1-hour bearer token per session; Rebel refreshes it automatically. All tools are read-only.

tools.name: get_daily_extended
tools.description:
Daily P&L-style detail: accounts × amounts for a data set across a date range.

REQUIRED: site_tag, data_set_id, begin_date, end_date.

RESPONSE SIZE: can be large. Prefer a single site_tag and a ≤31-day range. Defaults exclude zero rows and totals; set include_zeroes=true / include_totals=true to override.

RETURNS: { rows: [...] }

tools.name: get_daily_labor
tools.description:
Daily labor detail by employee and account for a site and date range.

REQUIRED: begin_date, end_date, site_tag.

Dates accept YYYY-MM-DD. Use "ALL" or a specific siteTag (from list_sites). For a single day, set end_date equal to begin_date.

RETURNS: { rows: [{ siteTag, siteName, personID, date, employee ID, hours, amounts, type, label }] }

tools.name: get_ledger_batches
tools.description:
GL ledger batches for a site and date range.

REQUIRED: site_tag, begin_date, end_date, status, type_id.

- status: "E" (Exported), "S" (Submitted), or "ALL".
- type_id: a specific type or "ALL".
- site_tag: specific tag, or "ALL" for all sites.

tools.name: get_monthly_extended
tools.description:
Monthly P&L detail for a data set across a month / year range.

REQUIRED: site_tag, data_set_id, year, begin_month, end_month.

Months are 1-12. end_year is optional; omit to stay within a single year.

RETURNS: { rows: [...] }

tools.name: get_sales_bookings
tools.description:
Sales bookings within a date range. site_tag is optional — omit or leave empty to pull all properties.

RETURNS: { rows: [{ bookingType, bookingID, siteTag, saleSysCode, resName, resAcctName, marketSeg, minDate, maxDate }] }

tools.name: get_sales_pace_events
tools.description:
Sales pace event-level data for a site and date range.

REQUIRED: as_of_date, begin_date, end_date, site_tag. Use site_tag="ALL" for all properties.

tools.name: get_sales_pace_rooms
tools.description:
Sales pace room-night data for a site and date range.

REQUIRED: as_of_date, begin_date, end_date, site_tag. Use site_tag="ALL" for all properties.

tools.name: get_sales_pace_transient
tools.description:
Sales pace transient (non-group) data for a site and date range.

REQUIRED: as_of_date, begin_date, end_date, site_tag. Use site_tag="ALL" for all properties.

RETURNS: { rows: [...] }

connectors.name: Vanta
connectors.description: Vanta compliance management: monitor and update vulnerabilities, tests, controls, evidence, resources, people, vendors, and compliance summary. Read and write SOC2/ISO27001/HIPAA compliance posture.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. Select your Vanta region below (US if unsure)
2. Click 'Open Vanta Developer Console'
3. Sign in to Vanta (if needed)
4. Click '+ Create' in the top right
5. Name it (e.g., 'Rebel'), select 'Manage Vanta', and click Create
6. Copy the OAuth Client ID and paste it below
7. Click 'Generate client secret', copy the secret, and paste it below

Note: Read and write access. Rebel can view and update your compliance posture including vendors, vulnerabilities, and evidence. If upgrading from a previous read-only setup, regenerate your OAuth credentials with the 'Manage Vanta' scope.

setupFields.label: Vanta Region
setupFields.helpText: US for app.vanta.com, EU for app.eu.vanta.com, AUS for app.aus.vanta.com

tools.name: vanta_get_compliance_summary
tools.description:
Get an aggregate compliance summary with pass/fail counts by framework.

WORKFLOW:
- Use this for a quick compliance posture overview.
- Optionally filter to one framework (e.g., SOC2).
- Aggregates up to 500 tests across 5 pages.

RELATED TOOLS:
- vanta_list_tests for detailed per-test data.
- vanta_list_controls for control-level posture.

RETURNS:
- JSON with ok, summary (frameworks, totalTests, passRate), and partial flag if truncated.

tools.name: vanta_get_control
tools.description:
Get details for one Vanta control by ID.

WORKFLOW:
- Call vanta_list_controls first to find the control_id.
- Use this for detailed control review and mapped test context.

RELATED TOOLS:
- vanta_list_controls to discover IDs.
- vanta_list_tests to inspect related tests.

RETURNS:
- JSON with ok and control.

tools.name: vanta_get_test
tools.description:
Get details for one Vanta compliance test by ID.

WORKFLOW:
- Call vanta_list_tests first to find the test_id.
- Use this to inspect evidence, status, and remediation context for one test.

RELATED TOOLS:
- vanta_list_tests to discover IDs.
- vanta_list_controls to review related controls.

RETURNS:
- JSON with ok and test.

tools.name: vanta_get_vulnerability
tools.description:
Get details for one Vanta vulnerability by ID.

WORKFLOW:
- Call vanta_list_vulnerabilities first to find the vulnerability_id.
- Use this when you need the full record for remediation or audit review.

RELATED TOOLS:
- vanta_list_vulnerabilities to discover IDs.

RETURNS:
- JSON with ok and vulnerability.

tools.name: vanta_list_controls
tools.description:
List Vanta controls with optional framework and status filters.

WORKFLOW:
- Use this to review SOC2, ISO27001, HIPAA, or other framework control posture.
- Use page_size and page_cursor for pagination.

RELATED TOOLS:
- vanta_get_control for one control's details.
- vanta_list_tests to inspect test status behind controls.

RETURNS:
- JSON with ok, controls, count, and pageInfo.

tools.name: vanta_list_evidence
tools.description:
List evidence items from Vanta with optional type and status filters.

WORKFLOW:
- Use this to review uploaded evidence for compliance audits.
- Use page_size and page_cursor for pagination.

RELATED TOOLS:
- vanta_list_tests to see which tests reference this evidence.

RETURNS:
- JSON with ok, evidence, count, and pageInfo.

tools.name: vanta_list_people

List people tracked in Vanta with optional role and status filters.

WORKFLOW:
- Use this to review employees, contractors, and their compliance status.
- Use page_size and page_cursor for pagination.

RELATED TOOLS:
- vanta_list_resources for non-people resources.

RETURNS:
- JSON with ok, people, count, and pageInfo.

tools.name: vanta_list_resources
tools.description:
List monitored resources from Vanta with an optional resource_type filter.

WORKFLOW:
- Use this to inspect computers, cloud accounts, repositories, and SaaS applications tracked by Vanta.
- Use page_size and page_cursor for pagination.

RELATED TOOLS:
- vanta_list_tests to see compliance test status.
- vanta_list_vulnerabilities to review vulnerability posture.

RETURNS:
- JSON with ok, resources, count, and pageInfo.

tools.name: vanta_list_tests
tools.description:
List compliance tests from Vanta with optional status and framework filters.

WORKFLOW:
- Use this to find passing, failing, disabled, or framework-specific tests.
- Use page_size and page_cursor for pagination.

RELATED TOOLS:
- vanta_get_test for full details on one test.
- vanta_list_controls to inspect mapped controls.

RETURNS:
- JSON with ok, tests, count, and pageInfo.

tools.name: vanta_list_vulnerabilities

List vulnerabilities from Vanta with optional severity, status, and service filters.

WORKFLOW:
- Start here to review open vulnerability posture.
- Use page_size and page_cursor for pagination.

RELATED TOOLS:
- vanta_get_vulnerability for full details on one vulnerability.

RETURNS:
- JSON with ok, vulnerabilities, count, and pageInfo.

Query Vanta test results with flexible filters including date range.

WORKFLOW:
- Use this for time-scoped compliance queries (e.g., 'tests that failed this month').
- Use page_size and page_cursor for pagination.

RELATED TOOLS:
- vanta_list_tests for simpler test listing.
- vanta_get_compliance_summary for aggregate pass/fail rates.

RETURNS:

tools.name: vanta_list_vendors
tools.description:
List vendors tracked in Vanta with optional category and status filters.

WORKFLOW:
- Use this to review your third-party vendor inventory and compliance posture.
- Use page_size and page_cursor for pagination.

RELATED TOOLS:
- vanta_get_vendor for full details on one vendor.
- vanta_create_vendor to add a new vendor.

RETURNS:
- JSON with ok, vendors, count, and pageInfo.

tools.name: vanta_get_vendor
tools.description:
Get details for one Vanta vendor by ID.

WORKFLOW:
- Call vanta_list_vendors first to find the vendor_id.
- Use this to inspect vendor details, risk level, and compliance documentation.

RELATED TOOLS:
- vanta_list_vendors to discover IDs.
- vanta_update_vendor to modify vendor fields.
- vanta_attach_vendor_document to attach compliance documents.

RETURNS:
- JSON with ok and vendor.

tools.name: vanta_create_vendor
tools.description:
Create a new vendor in Vanta.

WORKFLOW:
- Use this to register a new third-party vendor in your compliance system.
- Provide vendor name, website, and category at minimum.

RELATED TOOLS:
- vanta_list_vendors to check existing vendors.
- vanta_attach_vendor_document to add documents after creation.

RETURNS:

Update an existing vendor's fields in Vanta.

WORKFLOW:
- Call vanta_get_vendor first to confirm the current state.

RELATED TOOLS:
- vanta_get_vendor to review before updating.
- vanta_list_vendors to discover vendor IDs.

RETURNS:
- JSON with ok and the updated vendor.

tools.name: vanta_attach_vendor_document
tools.description:
Attach a compliance document to an existing vendor in Vanta.

WORKFLOW:
- Use this to attach SOC2 reports, security questionnaires, or contracts to a vendor.
- Provide a URL to the document; Vanta stores the reference.

RELATED TOOLS:
- vanta_get_vendor to review vendor before attaching.
- vanta_create_vendor to create the vendor first if needed.

RETURNS:
- JSON with ok and the attached document.

tools.name: vanta_update_vulnerability
tools.description:
Update the status or remediation info for a Vanta vulnerability.

WORKFLOW:
- Call vanta_get_vulnerability first to confirm the current state.
- Use this to mark vulnerabilities as fixed, accepted, or to add remediation notes.

RELATED TOOLS:
- vanta_list_vulnerabilities to discover vulnerability IDs.
- vanta_get_vulnerability to review before updating.

RETURNS:
- JSON with ok and the updated vulnerability.

tools.name: vanta_upload_document
tools.description:
Upload an evidence document to Vanta for compliance audits.

WORKFLOW:
- Use this to add new evidence documents (policies, procedures, reports).
- Provide a URL to the document; Vanta stores the reference.

RELATED TOOLS:
- vanta_list_evidence to review existing evidence.

RETURNS:
- JSON with ok and the uploaded document.

connectors.name: Kling AI
connectors.description: Kling AI video generation. Create 5-10 second videos from text prompts or still images. Be specific about motion, camera angle, and style. Generation takes 2-5 minutes. 'Create a video of ocean waves crashing on rocks at sunset, slow motion, cinematic' or 'Animate this product photo with a gentle zoom and rotating light'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. Click 'Open Kling AI' below to go to Developer Console
2. Click '+ Create a new API Key'
3. Copy both the Access Key and Secret Key
4. Paste them here - you need BOTH keys

tools.name: check_kling_task
tools.description:
Check if a Kling video generation task is complete.

WHEN TO CALL:
- After generate_kling_video or generate_kling_image_to_video returns a task_id
- Wait ~30 seconds between checks
- Keep polling until status is "succeed" or "failed"

RESPONSE STATUS VALUES:
- "submitted" → Task received, generation starting
- "processing" → Video being generated (wait and poll again)
- "succeed" → DONE! Response includes video.url (download/share this)
- "failed" → Error occurred, check the error message

TYPICAL TIMELINE:
- 0:00 - Submit task, get task_id
- 0:30 - First check: usually "processing"
- 1:00-3:00 - Keep checking every 30s
- 2:00-5:00 - Usually complete

VIDEO URL: Valid for 30 days after generation. Save/download promptly if needed long-term.

tools.name: configure_kling_api_keys
tools.description:
Save Kling API credentials. Call this when the user provides their API keys.

WHEN TO USE:
- User says "here are my Kling keys" or provides access_key/secret_key
- You get an AUTH_REQUIRED error from other Kling tools
- User wants to update/change their Kling credentials

WHERE TO GET KEYS:
Direct user to: https://app.klingai.com/global/dev/api-key
1. Sign in to Kling AI
2. Go to API Keys section
3. Create new API key
4. Copy BOTH the Access Key and Secret Key

IMPORTANT: Both keys are required. The Access Key identifies the account, the Secret Key signs requests.

tools.name: generate_kling_image_to_video
tools.description:
Animate a still image into a video using AI.

WORKFLOW:
1. You need a PUBLIC image URL (must start with https://)
2. Call this tool with the image URL and a motion prompt → returns task_id
3. Wait 30 seconds, then call check_kling_task(task_id, task_type="image2video")
4. Repeat step 3 until status is "succeed" (typically 2-5 minutes)

IMAGE REQUIREMENTS:
- Must be publicly accessible (https:// URL)
- High resolution works best
- Clear subject for animation

MOTION PROMPT TIPS (describe HOW to animate):
- Camera motion: "camera slowly zooms in", "camera pans left to right", "dolly forward"
- Subject motion: "person turns head and smiles", "hair blowing in wind", "eyes blink naturally"
- Environment: "clouds drift across sky", "leaves rustle", "water ripples"

EXAMPLE MOTION PROMPTS:
- "Gentle zoom in on face, subtle smile forms, hair moves slightly in breeze"
- "Camera slowly orbits around the product, studio lighting shifts"
- "Clouds move across sky, water in lake gently ripples, birds fly in distance"

IMPORTANT: If you have a local image, you must first upload it to a hosting service (Imgur, Cloudinary, etc.) to get a public URL.

tools.name: generate_kling_video
tools.description:
Create an AI-generated video from a text description.

WORKFLOW:
1. Call this tool with your prompt → returns task_id immediately
2. Wait 30 seconds, then call check_kling_task(task_id, task_type="text2video")
3. Repeat step 2 until status is "succeed" (typically 2-5 minutes total)
4. When complete, you'll get a video URL (valid for 30 days)

PROMPT TIPS (be specific!):
- Describe the SCENE: "a golden retriever playing in autumn leaves"
- Specify MOTION: "running towards camera", "slow motion", "timelapse"
- Add CAMERA: "close-up", "wide shot", "camera pans left to right"
- Include STYLE: "cinematic lighting", "documentary style", "animated"
- Set MOOD: "warm sunset glow", "dramatic storm clouds", "peaceful morning"

EXAMPLE PROMPTS:
- "A cup of coffee with steam rising, close-up shot, morning light through window, cozy atmosphere, slow motion"
- "Ocean waves crashing on rocky shore at sunset, wide angle, cinematic, dramatic golden hour lighting"
- "Modern city skyline at night, timelapse, cars leaving light trails, aerial view"

MODELS (newest to oldest):
- kling-v2-6 (default): Latest model with best quality and native audio support
- kling-v2-5-turbo: Faster generation, still high quality
- kling-v2-master: High quality model
- kling-v2-1-master: Previous generation master
- kling-v1-6: Good balance of speed and quality
- kling-v1: Original model

COSTS: ~100 credits for 5s standard, ~200 for 5s pro. Check balance at https://app.klingai.com/global/dev/billing

connectors.name: Runway ML
connectors.description: AI media generation via Runway. Video (image-to-video, text-to-video, video-to-video, character performance), image generation, text-to-speech, sound effects, voice dubbing, and voice isolation. Supports local files directly. 'Animate this image' or 'Create a video of ocean waves at sunset'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. Click 'Open Runway' below to go to the Developer Portal
2. Create an account and organization if you don't have one
3. Go to API Keys and generate a new key
4. Add credits in the Billing tab (minimum $10 = 1000 credits)
5. Paste your API key here

tools.name: cancel_runway_task
tools.description: Cancel a pending/running task or delete a completed task. Saves credits if cancelled before completion.

tools.name: character_performance
tools.description:
Animate a character with facial expressions and body movements from a reference performance video (Act-Two).

USE CASES: Make a character talk/emote, lip-sync, animate an avatar, bring a portrait to life.

HOW IT WORKS: Provide a CHARACTER (image/video with visible face) + REFERENCE PERFORMANCE (3-30s video of a person performing). The model maps the performance onto the character.

MODEL: act_two. 5 credits/sec. Output duration matches reference video length.

WORKFLOW: Returns task_id → poll with check_runway_task (or use wait_for_runway_task) → get output URL.

tools.name: check_runway_task
tools.description:
Check the status of any Runway generation task. Poll every 10-30 seconds.

STATUS VALUES: PENDING → THROTTLED → RUNNING → SUCCEEDED (output URLs) or FAILED.

TYPICAL WAIT TIMES: Images: 10-30s, Audio: 10-30s, Videos: 1-5 minutes.

TIP: Use wait_for_runway_task instead for automatic polling.

tools.name: configure_runway_api_key
tools.description: Save your Runway API key. Get one at https://dev.runwayml.com/ → API Keys. Add credits in Billing (min $10 = 1000 credits).

tools.name: create_custom_voice
tools.description:
Create a custom voice from a text description of desired voice characteristics.

MODELS: eleven_multilingual_ttv_v2 (default), eleven_ttv_v3 (newer).

The voice takes a few seconds to process. Check status with list_custom_voices. Once READY, use its ID with generate_speech.

EXAMPLE: name="Corporate Narrator", prompt="A warm, authoritative male voice with a slight British accent, professional but approachable"

tools.name: delete_custom_voice
tools.description: Delete a custom voice by ID. This is permanent and cannot be undone.

tools.name: download_runway_output
tools.description:
Download a Runway output (video, image, audio) to a local file. Use after a task succeeds to save the output locally.

WORKFLOW: generate_* → wait_for_runway_task → download_runway_output with the output URL.

tools.name: dub_audio
tools.description:
Translate and dub audio into a different language, cloning the original speaker's voice.

SUPPORTED LANGUAGES: en, hi, pt, zh, es, fr, de, ja, ar, ru, ko, id, it, nl, tr, pl, sv, fil, ms, ro, uk, el, cs, da, fi, bg, hr, sk, ta.

COST: 1 credit per 2 seconds. WORKFLOW: Returns task_id → poll or use wait_for_runway_task.

tools.name: generate_image
tools.description:
Generate an image from text, optionally with reference images for style/content guidance.

USE CASES: Product variations, style transfer, character design, marketing assets, concept art.

MODELS:
  gen4_image       - HIGH QUALITY. 5 credits/720p, 8 credits/1080p. Best detail and adherence.
  gen4_image_turbo - FAST & CHEAP. 2 credits/image at any resolution. Good for iterations.
  gemini_2.5_flash - Google Gemini Flash via Runway. 5 credits/image. Fast, different aesthetic.

REFERENCE IMAGES (optional, 1-3):
  - Tag them with a short label (e.g., "cat", "style_ref")
  - Use @tag in your prompt to reference specific images
  - Example: prompt "@character standing in @background" with images tagged "character" and "background"

LOCAL FILES SUPPORTED for reference images.

WORKFLOW: Returns task_id → poll with check_runway_task every 10s (or use wait_for_runway_task) → get output URL.

tools.name: generate_sound_effect
tools.description:
Generate sound effects from a text description using ElevenLabs via Runway.

EXAMPLES: "Thunderstorm with heavy rain", "Spaceship engine humming", "Crowd cheering in a stadium".

COST: 1 credit per 6 seconds. WORKFLOW: Returns task_id → poll or use wait_for_runway_task.

tools.name: generate_speech
tools.description:
Generate spoken audio from text using ElevenLabs voices via Runway.

VOICES (49 presets): Maya, Arjun, Serene, Bernard, Billy, Mark, Clint, Mabel, Chad, Leslie, Eleanor, Elias, Elliot, Grungle, Brodie, Sandra, Kirk, Kylie, Lara, Lisa, Malachi, Marlene, Martin, Miriam, Monster, Paula, Pip, Rusty, Ragnar, Xylar, Maggie, Jack, Katie, Noah, James, Rina, Ella, Mariah, Frank, Claudia, Niki, Vincent, Kendrick, Myrna, Tom, Wanda, Benjamin, Kiana, Rachel.

You can also use custom voice IDs from list_custom_voices or create_custom_voice.

COST: 1 credit per 50 characters. WORKFLOW: Returns task_id → poll or use wait_for_runway_task.

tools.name: generate_video_from_image
tools.description:
Animate a still image into a video. Supports first-frame (and optionally last-frame) keyframe control.

USE CASES: Product shots, character animation, scene animation, social media content, transitions between two images.

MODELS:
  gen4.5      - FLAGSHIP. Best quality, strong prompt adherence, cinematic. Supports keyframe array. 12 credits/sec.
  gen4_turbo  - FAST & CHEAP. Subtle motion (parallax, camera, wind/water). Cannot create new poses. 5 credits/sec.
  gen3a_turbo - LEGACY. Older model, cheaper. 5 credits/sec.
  veo3.1      - BEST QUALITY (third-party). Full animation + generated audio. 40 credits/sec (audio), 20 credits/sec (no audio).
  veo3.1_fast - GOOD BALANCE. Same as veo3.1 but faster/cheaper. 15 credits/sec (audio), 10 credits/sec (no audio).
  veo3        - Previous generation. 40 credits/sec.

KEYFRAME SUPPORT: Provide prompt_image as the first frame. Optionally provide last_frame_image to control where the video ends up — the model will interpolate between the two images.

IMPORTANT: gen4_turbo/gen3a_turbo CANNOT make characters wave, talk, or change expression. Use gen4.5, veo3.1, or act_two for that.

LOCAL FILES SUPPORTED: Pass local file paths (auto-uploaded for large files via ephemeral upload API).

WORKFLOW: Returns task_id → poll with check_runway_task every 30s (or use wait_for_runway_task) → get output URL.

COST EXAMPLES (1 credit = $0.01):
  gen4.5 5s = 60 credits ($0.60)  |  gen4_turbo 5s = 25 credits ($0.25)
  veo3.1 8s = 320 credits ($3.20) |  veo3.1_fast 8s = 120 credits ($1.20)

tools.name: generate_video_from_text
tools.description:
Create a video entirely from a text description, no input image needed.

USE CASES: Original content creation, concept visualization, storytelling, ads.

MODELS:
  gen4.5      - FLAGSHIP. Cinematic quality, strong prompt adherence, 2-10s. 12 credits/sec.
  veo3.1      - BEST QUALITY (third-party). Video + audio from text. 40 credits/sec (audio) / 20 credits/sec (no audio).
  veo3.1_fast - GOOD BALANCE. Faster/cheaper. 15 credits/sec (audio) / 10 credits/sec (no audio).
  veo3        - Previous generation. 40 credits/sec.

PROMPT TIPS: Be specific about subject, action, camera, environment, and style.

WORKFLOW: Returns task_id → poll with check_runway_task (or use wait_for_runway_task) → get output URL.

tools.name: generate_video_from_video
tools.description:
Re-style or transform an existing video using Gen-4 Aleph.

USE CASES: Style transfer, brand consistency, creative effects, video-to-video transformation.

MODEL: gen4_aleph (only option). 15 credits/sec.

HOW IT WORKS: Source video provides motion/structure, text prompt describes transformation, optional reference image guides style.

WORKFLOW: Returns task_id → poll with check_runway_task (or use wait_for_runway_task) → get output URL.

tools.name: get_runway_balance
tools.description: Check your Runway credit balance, usage tier limits, and today's usage by model. 1 credit = $0.01.

tools.name: isolate_voice
tools.description: Isolate voice from background audio. Extracts clean speech. Input must be 4.6s-3600s. COST: 1 credit per 6 seconds.

tools.name: list_custom_voices
tools.description: List all custom voices you've created. Returns voice IDs, names, descriptions, and status (PROCESSING/READY/FAILED). Use the voice ID with generate_speech.

tools.name: preview_voice
tools.description: Generate a short audio preview of a voice from a text description, without creating it. Use this to audition voice characteristics before committing with create_custom_voice.

tools.name: query_credit_usage
tools.description: Query detailed credit usage broken down by model and day. Supports date ranges up to 90 days.

tools.name: swap_voice
tools.description:
Replace the voice in an audio or video file with a different voice, preserving speech content.

INPUT: Audio (.mp3, .wav) or video (.mp4). Local files supported. COST: 1 credit per 3 seconds.

tools.name: upload_media
tools.description:
Upload a local file to Runway's ephemeral storage, returning a runway:// URI that can be used in any generation tool. Supports files up to 200MB.

USE CASES: Upload large files that exceed data URI limits (images > 3.3MB, video/audio > 12MB), or pre-upload files you'll use in multiple generations (URI valid for 24 hours).

NOTE: Most tools auto-upload large local files automatically. Use this tool explicitly when you want to pre-upload or need the runway:// URI.

tools.name: wait_for_runway_task
tools.description:
Submit a task ID and wait for it to complete. Polls automatically until the task succeeds, fails, or times out. Much more convenient than manual polling with check_runway_task.

Returns the final task result including output URLs when successful.

connectors.name: Looker
connectors.description: Query Looker dashboards, explores, and data models. Business intelligence and analytics.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. In Looker Admin, go to Users > your user > Edit API3 Keys
2. Click 'New API3 Key' to create credentials
3. Copy the Client ID and Client Secret
4. Enter your Looker instance URL and credentials below

Note: Requires admin access to generate API credentials. Requires Google Cloud APIs enabled (Gemini Data Analytics, Cloud AI Companion).

3. Complete the browser OAuth flow with your Google account
4. Enter your GCP Project ID below

Note: Your Google account needs BigQuery User role (roles/bigquery.user) on the project. Requires Application Default Credentials configured.

3. Click 'Generate New Token' and name it (e.g., 'Rebel AI')
4. Copy the token immediately — you won't see it again!
5. Enter your workspace URL (including https://) and token below

3. For local: mongodb://localhost:27017/mydb
4. Replace <password> placeholder with your actual password
5. Paste your connection string below

Note: Read-only mode enabled by default for safety.

connectors.name: Browser Automation
connectors.description: Browser automation with persistent sessions. Navigate, click, fill forms, take screenshots, extract data using accessibility snapshots. Auto-detects Chrome/Brave. Use ONLY when explicitly asked or when no API/MCP exists for the target site. 'Take a screenshot of my LinkedIn profile' or 'Fill out this web form'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
Browser Automation opens a real browser window so you can watch Rebel work — every click, every keystroke. Sessions persist between uses, so logins stick.

If you'd rather Rebel work out of sight, untick 'Show the browser window' below.

First time using a logged-in service:
1. Ask Rebel to authenticate with the website (a browser opens)
2. Log in manually
3. Close the browser — Rebel saves the session for next time

Requires Google Chrome or Brave to be installed.

connectors.name: Replit
connectors.description: Manage Replit projects over SSH. Read, write, and list files in running Replit projects. Set up SSH keys and test connections — no terminal needed. 'Push my task brief to the Replit project' or 'Show me what Replit built'.
connectors.setupInstructions: Enable the connector, then ask Rebel to set up Replit SSH — it will generate keys and guide you through adding them to your Replit account.

tools.name: replit_check_connection
tools.description: Check SSH connectivity to a Replit project. Verifies the connection works and reports the working directory, SFTP support, and server information. Use this to validate setup before file operations. Set verbose=true for detailed diagnostics when troubleshooting auth or connection failures.

tools.name: replit_list_files
tools.description: List files and directories in a Replit project path. Returns file names, sizes, and types (file/directory). Use "." for root or specify a subdirectory path.

tools.name: replit_read_file
tools.description: Read the contents of a file from a Replit project. Returns the file content as text. For binary files, returns base64-encoded content.

tools.name: replit_setup_ssh
tools.description: Set up SSH keys and configuration for connecting to Replit projects. Generates an Ed25519 key pair, configures ~/.ssh/config, and provides the public key for the user to add to their Replit account. Safe to run multiple times — skips steps already completed.

tools.name: replit_write_file
tools.description: Write content to a file in a Replit project. Creates parent directories if needed. Uses atomic write (temp file + rename) and verifies the write by reading back. For binary files (images, etc.), pass base64-encoded content with encoding "base64".

connectors.name: Humaans
connectors.description: Humaans HR platform. List employees, view org chart, check job roles and departments, see who's on leave, request time off. 'Who's on the engineering team?' or 'Show me Sarah's job history' or 'Request PTO for next Friday'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. Click 'Open Humaans' below to go to token settings
2. Click 'Generate new token'
3. Name it (e.g., 'Rebel')
4. Select scopes: public:read, private:read, private:write
5. Copy the token
6. Paste the token here

tools.name: configure_humaans_api_key
tools.description:
Configure the Humaans API access token. Call this when the user provides their token.

Get a token from https://app.humaans.io/settings/home?tokens=1:
1. Click "Generate new token"
2. Name it (e.g., "Rebel")
3. Select scopes: public:read, private:read, private:write
4. Copy the token

tools.name: create_humaans_time_away
tools.description:
Create a time away request in Humaans (PTO, sick leave, etc.).

WORKFLOW - To request time off:
1. Call get_humaans_me to get your personId
2. Call list_humaans_time_away_types to find the timeAwayTypeId (e.g., "Paid time off")
3. Call this tool with the details

Dates must be in YYYY-MM-DD format. Use startPeriod/endPeriod for half days.

Example: { "personId": "VMB1...", "startDate": "2024-03-15", "endDate": "2024-03-15", "timeAwayTypeId": "abc123", "note": "Doctor appointment", "startPeriod": "am" }

COMMON MISTAKES:
- Don't guess timeAwayTypeId - always get it from list_humaans_time_away_types first
- Dates must be YYYY-MM-DD, not ISO datetime with time/timezone

tools.name: get_humaans_company
get_humaans_job_role
get_humaans_me
tools.description:
Get the current authenticated user's profile from Humaans.

Returns: name, email, job title, department, teams, location, status.
Use this to get your own personId for other operations (e.g., creating time away).

RELATED TOOLS:
- create_humaans_time_away: Use the returned id as personId
- list_humaans_time_away: Filter by your personId

tools.name: get_humaans_person
tools.description:
Get full employee profile from Humaans by their ID.

Returns detailed profile including: name, email, job role, department, teams,
location, employment dates, contract type, working days, manager, bio, social links.
Sensitive fields (tax ID, personal email, home address) are redacted for privacy.

Example: { "personId": "VMB1yzL5uL8VvNNCJc9rykJz" }

WORKFLOW - To find a person:
1. Call list_humaans_people to search (filter by email or team)
2. Use the person's id from the results here

tools.name: list_humaans_job_roles
tools.description:
List job role history for employees in Humaans.

Each person can have multiple job roles over time. The role with the most recent
effectiveDate that is not in the future is the current active role.

Use the asOf parameter to find what role someone had at a specific date.

Example: { "personId": "VMB1yzL5uL8VvNNCJc9rykJz" }
Example: { "personId": "VMB1yzL5uL8VvNNCJc9rykJz", "asOf": "2024-06-15" }

RELATED TOOLS:
- list_humaans_people: Find personId to filter by

tools.name: list_humaans_locations
tools.description:

tools.name: list_humaans_people

List employees from Humaans HR.

By default returns only active employees. Use status filter for other groups.
Returns compact summaries: id, name, email, job title, department, teams, location, status.

tools.name: list_humaans_time_away
tools.description:
List time away (PTO, sick leave, etc.) entries from Humaans.

Filter by person, date range, or approval status. Returns: dates, type, days count, approval status, notes.

Example: { "personId": "VMB1yzL5uL8VvNNCJc9rykJz" }
Example: { "startDateAfter": "2024-01-01", "startDateBefore": "2024-12-31" }

RELATED TOOLS:

tools.name: list_humaans_time_away_types

RELATED TOOLS:

connectors.name: Workday
connectors.description: Workday HCM platform: search workers, view employee profiles, browse org structure. 'Who works in engineering?' or 'Show me Alice's profile' or 'List our departments'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
To connect Workday, you'll need an admin to set up API access:

1. Create an Integration System User (ISU) in Workday
2. Add the ISU to an Integration Security Group with Worker Data access
3. Register an API Client (Tenant Setup > API Clients)
4. Note the Client ID and Client Secret
5. Optionally, generate a Refresh Token
6. Enter your Workday host domain (e.g., wd5-impl-services1.workday.com), tenant name, and credentials below

Note: Requires a Workday HCM subscription with REST API access enabled.

tools.name: configure_workday_credentials
tools.description:
Configure Workday API credentials. Call this when the user provides their Workday OAuth credentials.

SETUP PREREQUISITES:
1. A Workday Integration System User (ISU) with appropriate security group access
2. An API Client registered in Workday (Tenant Setup > API Clients)
3. The Client ID and Client Secret from the API Client registration
4. Optionally, a pre-generated Refresh Token (from OAuth token exchange)

PARAMETERS:
- host: Workday API domain (e.g., "wd5-impl-services1.workday.com")
- tenant: Your Workday tenant name (e.g., "acme_corp")
- client_id: OAuth Client ID from API Client registration
- client_secret: OAuth Client Secret
- refresh_token: (Optional) Pre-generated refresh token. If omitted, uses client_credentials grant.

WORKFLOW:
1. Ask the user for their Workday host domain and tenant name
2. Ask for their API Client credentials (Client ID + Secret)
3. Ask if they have a refresh token (optional)
4. Call this tool to validate and save

COMMON MISTAKES:
- Host should be just the domain (e.g., "wd5-impl-services1.workday.com"), not a full URL
- Tenant name is case-sensitive
- The ISU must have permissions for the REST API resources you want to access

tools.name: get_workday_worker
tools.description:
Get a worker's full profile by ID from Workday.

Returns detailed profile: name, email, title, manager status, location,
supervisory organization, years of service.

Example: { "worker_id": "3aa5550b7fe348b98d7b5741afc65534" }

WORKFLOW - To find a worker:
1. Call list_workday_workers to search by name or email
2. Use the worker's id from the results here

RELATED TOOLS:
- list_workday_workers: Search/browse workers to find IDs
- list_workday_organizations: See org structure

tools.name: list_workday_organizations
tools.description:
List organizations (departments, supervisory orgs, cost centers, etc.) in Workday.

Returns: ID, name/descriptor, type, active status.

Example: {}
Example: { "limit": 25, "offset": 50 }

Pagination: Returns up to 'limit' results (default 50, max 100). Use 'offset' for next page.

RELATED TOOLS:
- list_workday_workers: Browse workers in the organization
- get_workday_worker: See which organization a worker belongs to

tools.name: list_workday_workers
tools.description:
List or search workers (employees and contingent workers) in Workday.

Returns compact worker summaries: ID, name, email, title, manager status.

Example: {}
Example: { "search": "Jane Smith" }
Example: { "limit": 20, "offset": 100 }

Pagination: Returns up to 'limit' results (default 50, max 100). Use 'offset' for next page.

RELATED TOOLS:
- get_workday_worker: Pass a worker's id to get their full profile
- list_workday_organizations: Browse organizational structure

COMMON MISTAKES:
- search is a free-text filter (name, email, etc.) — not a Workday query language
- Maximum limit is 100 per request; use offset for pagination

connectors.name: QuickBooks Online
connectors.description: QuickBooks Online accounting. View and create invoices, bills, customers, vendors, employees, accounts, and more. Search with flexible queries. 'Show my unpaid invoices' or 'Create an invoice for Acme Corp' or 'List expense accounts'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
QuickBooks Online connects via the Intuit Developer API. You'll need four pieces of information:

1. Click 'Open QuickBooks' below to visit the Intuit Developer Portal
2. Create an app (or select existing) → go to Keys & credentials
3. Copy the Client ID and Client Secret
4. Use the OAuth Playground (in the portal) to generate a Refresh Token
5. Find your Company ID in the URL when logged into QuickBooks Online (the number after /app/)
6. Enter all four values below

Note: Refresh tokens expire after 100 days of inactivity. If you get auth errors later, generate a new refresh token and reconnect.

tools.name: configure_quickbooks
tools.description:
Configure QuickBooks Online credentials. Call this when the user provides their Intuit Developer app credentials.

WORKFLOW:
1. Go to https://developer.intuit.com/ and create an app (or use existing)
2. Get the Client ID and Client Secret from the app's Keys & credentials page
3. Add http://localhost:8000/callback as a Redirect URI
4. Use the OAuth Playground or your app's auth flow to obtain a Refresh Token
5. Find your Company ID (Realm ID) in the URL when logged into QuickBooks Online

COMMON MISTAKES:
- Refresh tokens expire after 100 days of inactivity — re-authenticate if you get auth errors
- The Realm ID is NOT the same as the Client ID — it's your company identifier
- Sandbox and Production use different credentials

tools.name: create_quickbooks_bill
tools.description:
Create a new bill (accounts payable) in QuickBooks Online.

Example: { "vendorId": "123", "lines": [{ "description": "Office supplies", "amount": 250, "accountId": "456" }] }

WORKFLOW:
1. Use list_quickbooks_vendors to find the vendor ID
2. Use list_quickbooks_accounts to find expense account IDs
3. Create with line items

tools.name: create_quickbooks_customer
create_quickbooks_invoice
tools.description:
Create a new invoice in QuickBooks Online.

Example: { "customerId": "123", "lines": [{ "description": "Consulting services", "amount": 1500, "detailType": "SalesItemLineDetail" }] }

WORKFLOW:
1. Use list_quickbooks_customers to find the customer ID
2. Create with line items (description + amount required)
3. Optionally set dueDate and memo

COMMON MISTAKES:
- customerId is required (use list_quickbooks_customers to find it)
- Each line needs at least description and amount
- Dates use YYYY-MM-DD format

tools.name: create_quickbooks_vendor
get_quickbooks_entity
tools.description:
Get a single QuickBooks entity by type and ID.

Example: { "entityType": "Invoice", "entityId": "123" }
Example: { "entityType": "Customer", "entityId": "456" }

Supported entity types: Account, Bill, BillPayment, Customer, Employee, Estimate, Invoice, Item, JournalEntry, Purchase, Vendor

RELATED TOOLS:
- query_quickbooks: Search for entities
- list_quickbooks_invoices, list_quickbooks_customers, etc. for listing

tools.name: list_quickbooks_accounts
list_quickbooks_bills
tools.description:
List bills (accounts payable) from QuickBooks Online.

Returns: Id, DocNumber, TxnDate, DueDate, Balance, TotalAmt, VendorRef.

Example: {}
Example: { "vendorId": "123" }

RELATED TOOLS:
- list_quickbooks_vendors: Find vendor IDs
- create_quickbooks_bill: Create a new bill

tools.name: list_quickbooks_customers
list_quickbooks_employees
list_quickbooks_invoices
tools.description:
List invoices from QuickBooks Online.

Returns: Id, DocNumber, TxnDate, DueDate, Balance, TotalAmt, CustomerRef, Line items.

Example: {}
Example: { "status": "unpaid" }
Example: { "customerId": "123" }

WORKFLOW:
1. Call with no args to see recent invoices
2. Filter by status (unpaid/paid/overdue) or customer
3. Use get_quickbooks_entity for full invoice details

RELATED TOOLS:
- create_quickbooks_invoice: Create a new invoice
- list_quickbooks_customers: Find customer IDs

tools.name: list_quickbooks_vendors
query_quickbooks
tools.description:
Run a QuickBooks query using the QuickBooks Query Language.

Returns matching entities. QuickBooks uses a SQL-like query language.

Example: { "query": "SELECT * FROM Invoice WHERE Balance > '0' ORDERBY DueDate" }
Example: { "query": "SELECT * FROM Customer WHERE Active = true" }
Example: { "query": "SELECT * FROM Bill WHERE DueDate > '2024-01-01'" }

WORKFLOW:
1. Use this for flexible searches across any entity type
2. Entity names are PascalCase: Invoice, Customer, Vendor, Bill, Employee, etc.
3. String values use single quotes, dates use 'YYYY-MM-DD' format

RELATED TOOLS:
- list_quickbooks_invoices, list_quickbooks_customers, etc. for pre-built queries
- get_quickbooks_entity for fetching a single entity by ID

COMMON MISTAKES:
- Entity names are case-sensitive PascalCase (Invoice, not invoice)
- Use single quotes for string/date values, not double quotes
- LIKE operator uses % wildcard: DisplayName LIKE '%Smith%'

connectors.name: ServiceNow
connectors.description: ServiceNow ITSM platform. List, create, and update incidents and change requests. Search knowledge base articles. List users. 'Show my open incidents' or 'Create a P1 incident for the login outage' or 'Find KB articles about VPN setup'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. Log in to your ServiceNow instance as an admin
2. Enter your instance name (e.g., 'acme' for acme.service-now.com)
3. Enter your ServiceNow username and password
4. The account needs read/write access to incident, change_request, kb_knowledge, and sys_user tables

Note: For production use, consider creating a dedicated integration user with appropriate roles (itil, knowledge).

tools.name: configure_servicenow
tools.description:
Configure ServiceNow credentials. Call this when the user provides their instance name, username, and password.

WORKFLOW:
1. Log in to your ServiceNow instance as an admin
2. Your instance name is the subdomain part of your URL (e.g., "acme" for acme.service-now.com)
3. Enter your username and password
4. The account needs read/write access to incident, change_request, kb_knowledge, and sys_user tables

Note: For production use, consider creating a dedicated integration user with appropriate roles (itil, knowledge).

tools.name: create_servicenow_incident
tools.description:
Create a new incident in ServiceNow.

Returns: the created incident record.

Example: { "short_description": "VPN not connecting" }
Example: { "short_description": "Email server down", "urgency": "1", "impact": "1", "description": "All users unable to send/receive email since 9am" }

WORKFLOW:
1. Provide at minimum a short_description
2. Set urgency and impact to control priority (1=High, 2=Medium, 3=Low)
3. Optionally assign to a group or caller

RELATED TOOLS:
- list_servicenow_incidents: Verify the incident was created
- update_servicenow_incident: Update the incident after creation

COMMON MISTAKES:
- urgency and impact are strings "1", "2", "3" — not numbers
- assignment_group expects the group name, not sys_id (when using display values)

tools.name: get_servicenow_change_request
tools.description:
Get a single change request by number (e.g., CHG0010001) or sys_id.

Returns: full change request record with all fields.

Example: { "identifier": "CHG0010001" }

WORKFLOW:
1. Use list_servicenow_change_requests to find change numbers
2. Call this tool with the change number or sys_id

RELATED TOOLS:
- list_servicenow_change_requests: Find change requests by query

COMMON MISTAKES:
- Use the full change number including prefix (e.g., CHG0010001, not 10001)

tools.name: get_servicenow_incident
tools.description:
Get a single incident by number (e.g., INC0010001) or sys_id.

Returns: full incident record with all fields.

Example: { "identifier": "INC0010001" }
Example: { "identifier": "6816f79cc0a8016401c5a33be04be441" }

WORKFLOW:
1. Use list_servicenow_incidents to find incident numbers
2. Call this tool with the incident number or sys_id

RELATED TOOLS:
- list_servicenow_incidents: Find incidents by query
- update_servicenow_incident: Modify an incident

COMMON MISTAKES:
- Use the full incident number including prefix (e.g., INC0010001, not 10001)

tools.name: get_servicenow_knowledge_article
tools.description:
Get a full knowledge base article by sys_id or number.

Returns: full article record including the article body text.

Example: { "identifier": "KB0010001" }

WORKFLOW:
1. Use search_servicenow_knowledge to find articles
2. Call this tool with the article sys_id or number

RELATED TOOLS:
- search_servicenow_knowledge: Find articles by keyword or query

COMMON MISTAKES:
- Article body can be large HTML content — consider summarizing for the user

tools.name: list_servicenow_change_requests
tools.description:
List or search change requests in ServiceNow.

Returns: number, short_description, state, type, priority, assigned_to, start_date, end_date.

Example: {}
Example: { "query": "state=implement^type=normal" }

WORKFLOW:
1. Call with no query to see recent change requests
2. Use query parameter for filtering
3. Use get_servicenow_change_request for full details

RELATED TOOLS:
- get_servicenow_change_request: Get full details by change number

COMMON MISTAKES:
- Change request states differ from incident states
- Type values: "normal", "standard", "emergency"

tools.name: list_servicenow_incidents
tools.description:
List or search incidents in ServiceNow.

Returns: number, short_description, state, priority, assigned_to, sys_created_on, sys_updated_on, urgency, impact.

Example: {}
Example: { "query": "active=true^priority=1" }
Example: { "query": "assigned_to.name=John Smith", "limit": 10 }

WORKFLOW:
1. Call with no query to see recent incidents
2. Use query parameter with ServiceNow encoded query syntax for filtering
3. Use get_servicenow_incident to see full details of a specific incident

RELATED TOOLS:
- get_servicenow_incident: Get full details by incident number
- create_servicenow_incident: Create a new incident

COMMON MISTAKES:
- ServiceNow query syntax uses ^ as AND separator, not &
- Use display values for reference fields (e.g., assigned_to.name=John Smith)

tools.name: list_servicenow_users
tools.description:
List or search users in ServiceNow.

Returns: sys_id, user_name, first_name, last_name, email, title, department, active.

Example: {}
Example: { "query": "active=true^departmentLIKEengineering" }
Example: { "query": "user_name=john.smith" }

WORKFLOW:
1. Call with no query to list active users
2. Use query parameter to filter by name, department, etc.

RELATED TOOLS:
- list_servicenow_incidents: Find incidents assigned to a user
- create_servicenow_incident: Use user sys_id as caller_id

COMMON MISTAKES:
- user_name is the login username, not the display name
- For name searches, use first_nameLIKE or last_nameLIKE in the query

tools.name: search_servicenow_knowledge
tools.description:
Search knowledge base articles in ServiceNow.

Returns: number, short_description, sys_created_on, author, kb_knowledge_base, workflow_state. Does NOT include article body (use get_servicenow_knowledge_article for full text).

Example: { "query": "VPN setup" }
Example: { "query": "short_descriptionLIKEvpn^workflow_state=published" }

WORKFLOW:
1. Search with keywords or encoded query
2. Use get_servicenow_knowledge_article to read the full article

RELATED TOOLS:
- get_servicenow_knowledge_article: Get full article content including body text

COMMON MISTAKES:
- Simple text queries are converted to short_descriptionLIKE queries automatically
- For advanced filtering, use ServiceNow encoded query syntax directly

tools.name: update_servicenow_incident
tools.description:
Update an existing incident in ServiceNow.

Returns: the updated incident record.

Example: { "sys_id": "6816f79cc0a8016401c5a33be04be441", "state": "2", "assigned_to": "john.smith" }
Example: { "sys_id": "6816f79cc0a8016401c5a33be04be441", "short_description": "Updated description", "urgency": "1" }

WORKFLOW:
1. Use get_servicenow_incident to find the sys_id
2. Call this tool with the sys_id and fields to update

RELATED TOOLS:
- get_servicenow_incident: Get current state before updating
- list_servicenow_incidents: Find the incident to update

COMMON MISTAKES:
- sys_id is required, not the incident number — use get_servicenow_incident to find it
- State values are strings: "1" (New), "2" (In Progress), "3" (On Hold), "6" (Resolved), "7" (Closed)

connectors.name: RebelInbox
connectors.description: Manage user's Actions: add/update/remove tasks, list/query items, feedback examples, bulk archive/delete, check status/stats.

tools.name: rebel_inbox_add
tools.description:
Add a single action item to the user's Actions. For multiple items, use rebel_inbox_add_many instead.

Think of yourself as a Chief of Staff briefing a CEO. Your job is NOT to hand
them a to-do list — it's to hand them decision-ready materials they can approve
in under 2 minutes. The best action item is one the user reviews and sends, not
one they have to start from scratch.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
COMMUNICATION STYLE — BRIEF LIKE A CHIEF OF STAFF
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Write like you're briefing an executive who has 30 seconds to scan this:

• Title: Action-oriented, 5-12 words. Lead with verb or key noun.
  Include the person's name when delegating or following up.
  GOOD: "Send Arnon updated deck + cohort retention KPIs"
  GOOD: "Follow up with Trish Skoglund re: Fairwater pilot"
  BAD:  "I noticed you mentioned wanting to review the Q3 proposal"

• Text: Essential context only. What, why, when — no fluff.
  GOOD: "James asked for updated projections by EOD Friday. Deck is ready, needs your sign-off."
  BAD:  "During the meeting with James, we discussed several topics including the Series A..."

• Clarifying questions: One short question, not a paragraph.
  GOOD: "Formal or casual tone?"
  BAD:  "What tone would you like me to use for this email?"

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ITEM INTENT — WHAT DOES THE USER NEED TO DO?
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Before creating an item, determine the user's required action. This drives
which fields you set and how the item appears in Actions:

1. REVIEW & SEND (best case — lowest friction, highest completion rate)
   You have a complete deliverable. User reviews, edits 10%, hits send.
   → Set: draft (the full deliverable) + actions: [{ type: "execute" }]
   → Example: Email draft, Slack message, HubSpot record fields, briefing text

2. DECIDE (user must choose between options)
   You cannot proceed without the user's judgment on something specific.
   → Set: clarifyingQuestion (the specific decision) + actions: [{ type: "execute" }]
   → Example: "Volume discount or per-seat pricing for Intragen?", "Formal or friendly tone?"

3. DO (user must take an action you cannot take for them)
   The action requires the user personally — a call, a meeting, a judgment call.
   → Set: actions: [{ type: "execute" }] + detailed text explaining what to do
   → Example: "Call Sarah to discuss Q3 budget", "Review and sign DocuSign"

4. KNOW (pure information — USUALLY DOES NOT BELONG IN ACTIONS)
   Status updates, monitoring notes, FYI items with no action.
   → STOP: Route to Coach/memory instead. Only add to Actions if the user
     explicitly asked for it (e.g., "add this to my actions").
   → If you must add: no actions, no clarifyingQuestion. But strongly prefer
     routing to Coach — Actions is for things that need the user's hands.

RULE: If an item has no actions AND no clarifyingQuestion, ask yourself:
"Is there something the user needs to DO here?" If the answer is no,
route it to Coach/memory, not Actions — unless the user explicitly
requested it (e.g., "add this to my actions").

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
DRAFTS — ALWAYS DRAFT THE DELIVERABLE (THIS IS THE #1 RULE)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
A draft transforms a 30-minute task into a 2-minute review. ALWAYS attempt
a draft when the deliverable is predictable. The user sees a preview and
can approve with one click or request edits.

WHEN TO DRAFT (always, unless the deliverable is genuinely unpredictable):
  "Send email to X"          → draft = the full email body
  "Follow up with X"         → draft = the follow-up message
  "Slack X about Y"          → draft = the Slack message
  "Brief X on Y"             → draft = the briefing text
  "Create HubSpot record"    → draft = the record fields formatted
  "Post shoutout for X"      → draft = the shoutout message
  "Reply to X"               → draft = the reply
  "Draft proposal for X"     → draft = the proposal

WHEN NOT TO DRAFT (the deliverable requires user judgment to even start):
  "Decide between A and B"   → use clarifyingQuestion instead
  "Call Sarah"                → no deliverable to draft
  "Review contract"           → the contract IS the deliverable

DRAFT FORMAT: Write the actual artifact, not a description of it.
  BAD draft:  "An email to James about Q3 projections"
  GOOD draft: "Hi James,

Attached are the updated Q3 projections you requested..."

ACCURACY: Do not invent facts, recipient details, figures, or commitments in drafts.
  If key details are missing, use [placeholders] or set a clarifyingQuestion instead.
  A draft with "[amount TBD]" is better than one with a fabricated number.

AUTOMATION FLOWS: For automated/batch item creation (meeting transcripts, source
  capture), draft when the deliverable is clear from context. Skip drafting if you
  lack sufficient context — a low-quality filler draft creates more work than it saves.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
DELEGATION ITEMS — TRACK THE OPEN LOOP
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Nearly half of action items involve delegating to someone else. When creating
items that delegate work (send, brief, delegate, follow up, contact, DM),
structure them for tracking:

• ALWAYS include the person's name in the title
  GOOD: "Brief Alex on FICO multi-agent opportunity"
  BAD:  "Brief sales team on new opportunity"

• ALWAYS draft the deliverable (the message/email/brief you will send them)
  This is the most common missed draft — delegation items almost always have
  a predictable deliverable.

• Do NOT use relevantDate for delegation follow-up timing. relevantDate means
  "this item is stale after this date" and triggers auto-archival — the opposite
  of what you want for tracking open loops. Leave relevantDate unset for
  delegation items unless they are genuinely time-bound (e.g., event-related).

• If the delegation has already happened and you're tracking the response,
  set clarifyingQuestion to "Did [person] respond?" or "Was this completed?"

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
WHEN TO USE THIS TOOL
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
• User says: "add to actions", "remind me later", "save this for later"
• You've created a deliverable the user might want to send or share (posts, emails, prepared materials)
• You've identified a task that requires user input or action
• Following up on something from a meeting, email, or document
• Adding 1 item. For 2+ items, use rebel_inbox_add_many.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
PROHIBITED ITEM TYPES — DO NOT ADD THESE TO ACTIONS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Actions is for concrete tasks only. Route everything else to Coach/memory.

• System receipts and automation logs:
  "Actions enriched Wed 4 Mar — 26 items updated"
  "Daily cleanup receipt — Wednesday 4 March 2026"
  "Source capture complete — 15 sources processed"

• Status updates and monitoring notes (no action for the user):
  "HBSE — Whitney reviewing, Alex engaged" (awareness, not a task)
  "Monitor: Greg's brainstorm in progress" (FYI, not a task)

• Insights, learnings, wins, and recaps:
  "Insight: Chief of Staff page — high visits but zero interaction"
  "Win: 3+ connectors correlates with strong user retention"

• Confirmations that something is already done or resolved:
  "Deployment successful — all services green"
  "Bug fixed: login redirect now works"

If the user cannot DO something with this item right now, it does not
belong in Actions. Log it, write to memory, or route to Coach.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
EISENHOWER MATRIX — CHOOSING urgent AND important
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
┌─────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ DO NOW (urgent=true, important=true)│ SCHEDULE (urgent=false, important=true)│
│ Crises, deadlines, urgent requests  │ Strategic work, planning, goals      │
├─────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────┤
│ DELEGATE (urgent=true, important=false)│ CONSIDER (urgent=false, important=false)│
│ Interruptions, some emails, requests│ Nice-to-have, FYI, low priority      │
└─────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────┘
DEFAULT: urgent=false, important=true → "Schedule" (most tasks go here)

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
CATEGORY — ORIGIN/INTENT TRACKING
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ALWAYS set category. This is required for analytics and filtering:
• user-request: User explicitly asked ("add to actions", "remind me")
• automation: Running as part of a scheduled automation (daily briefing, source capture)
• meeting-action: Processing a meeting transcript
• follow-up: Derived from a conversation (not explicitly requested by user)
• system: System notifications (errors, warnings, service alerts)
If genuinely unsure, omit — defaults to "uncategorized". But try to categorize.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SOURCE/PROVENANCE — WHERE DID THIS COME FROM?
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Set "source" to track where this item originated:
• Scheduled automation: { kind: "automation", automationId: "<id>", automationName: "<name>" }
• Meeting transcript processing: { kind: "meeting", meetingTitle: "<title>" }
• Conversation follow-up: { kind: "conversation", sessionId: "<session-id>" }
• General text label: { kind: "text", label: "Morning Briefing" }
• Workspace file: { kind: "workspace", path: "/path/to/file" }
If you are an automation, ALWAYS set kind:"automation" with your automationId.
If processing a meeting, ALWAYS set kind:"meeting" with the meeting title.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
EMAIL REFERENCES — LINK ITEMS TO EMAIL THREADS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
When creating email-action items (reply to, follow up on email, respond to), ALWAYS include
an email reference with the threadId and provider so Rebel can track reply status:
  references: [{ kind: "email", threadId: "<thread-id>", messageId: "<message-id>", provider: "gmail" or "outlook", label: "Thread: <subject>" }]
The threadId and messageId come from the email provider's message data.
The provider field ("gmail" or "outlook") tells Rebel which tools to use for reply detection.
This enables automatic archival when the email is replied to.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
TONE GUIDELINES
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Professional and neutral — write like a calendar entry or project tracker.
• Never use informal verbs: "chase", "nag", "bug", "ping"
• Use "Follow up with" instead of "Chase", "Contact" instead of "Ping"
• No editorial commentary — state the action, not your opinion
• No exclamation marks in titles
  GOOD: "Follow up with Athena re: sales tax refund"
  BAD:  "Chase Athena on sales tax refund"
  GOOD: "Review Q3 proposal — deadline Friday"
  BAD:  "You really should look at that Q3 proposal!"

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
OWNER-RELEVANCE
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Only add items for actions OWNED BY or RELEVANT TO the current user.
Do not add other people's tasks. If the user was an observer (not the
assignee or decision-maker), do NOT add the item.
Ask: "Would this person need to act on this if they missed the meeting?"
If no, skip it.

If "Harry needs to fix connectors", that is Harry's task — do NOT add
it unless the user IS Harry or has a specific follow-up action.

SKIP EXAMPLES — do NOT add these:
• "Customer support team to handle billing dispute" (support's task)
• "ROI analysis shows strong prognosis" (insight, not action)
• "Engineering team will fix the connector issue" (engineering's task)
• "Sales team follow-up with prospect" (sales team's task)
• "[Person name] to send the deck by Friday" (that person's task)

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
CONTEXT SUFFICIENCY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Each item must have enough context to be actionable without re-reading
the source material. Include: what, who, when, and where it came from.
BAD:  "Define experiment framework: goals, analytics, deadline"
GOOD: "Define Rebel feature experiment framework — goals, analytics
       criteria, and deadline (discussed in Eng AI seminar Feb 25)"
If you can't make it specific enough, skip it — vague items waste
the user's time and get ignored.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
PARAMETERS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
title (required): 5-12 words, action-oriented. Include person name for delegation items.
text (optional): 1-3 sentences of context. Becomes the prompt when user clicks "Go".
source (optional): Where this came from — see SOURCE/PROVENANCE section above.
references (optional): Related files, URLs, or email threads.
actions (optional): Array of action objects. Set [{ type: "execute" }] for items where
  the user should act (Review & Send, Decide, Do intents). Also supports
  { type: "shareToSocial", text: "...", platforms: ["twitter"] } for social posts.
  Items without actions are treated as informational — see PROHIBITED ITEM TYPES.
urgent (optional, default false): Needs attention TODAY?
