tools.description: Update a credit note in Xero. Only works on draft credit notes.  All line items must be provided. Any line items not provided will be removed. Including existing line items.  Do not modify line items that have not been specified by the user. When a credit note is updated, a deep link to the credit note in Xero is returned. This deep link can be used to view the credit note in Xero directly. This link should be displayed to the user.

tools.name: update-invoice
tools.description: Update an invoice in Xero. Only works on draft invoices.  All line items must be provided. Any line items not provided will be removed. Including existing line items.  Do not modify line items that have not been specified by the user. When an invoice is updated, a deep link to the invoice in Xero is returned.  This deep link can be used to view the contact in Xero directly.  This link should be displayed to the user.

tools.name: update-item
tools.description: Update an item in Xero.

tools.name: update-manual-journal
tools.description: Update a manual journal in Xero. Only works on draft manual journals.  Do not modify line items or parameters that have not been specified by the user.

tools.name: update-quote
tools.description: Update a quote in Xero. Only works on draft quotes.  All line items must be provided. Any line items not provided will be removed. Including existing line items.  Do not modify line items that have not been specified by the user.  When a quote is updated, a deep link to the quote in Xero is returned.  This deep link can be used to view the quote in Xero directly.  This link should be displayed to the user.

tools.name: update-tracking-category

connectors.name: n8n (Official)
connectors.description: Build, run, and manage n8n workflows from natural language: search nodes, draft workflows via the SDK, test, publish, and manage data tables.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. n8n hosts the MCP server inside your own n8n instance — it's reachable at https://<your-n8n-domain>/mcp-server/http
2. Enter that URL below using your real n8n domain (Cloud, self-hosted, or community edition all work)
3. Click 'Set up with Rebel', then follow the OAuth sign-in in your browser

Notes:
- Your n8n instance must be on a recent version that includes the official MCP server (April 2026+).
- The MCP server respects your existing n8n permissions: you only get tools for what your account can already see and do.
- If OAuth doesn't complete, double-check that your n8n instance is reachable from this machine and that the MCP server endpoint is enabled.

tools.name: search_workflows
tools.description: Search for workflows with optional filters. Returns a preview of each workflow.

tools.name: get_workflow_details
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific workflow including trigger details.

tools.name: execute_workflow
tools.description: Execute a workflow by ID by mapping data from user prompt to trigger inputs. Returns execution ID and status. Performs full workflow execution, without mocking or skipping any nodes.

tools.name: get_execution
tools.description: Get execution details by execution ID and workflow ID. By default returns metadata only.

tools.name: test_workflow
tools.description: Test a workflow using pin data to bypass external services. Trigger nodes, nodes with credentials, and HTTP Request nodes are pinned; other nodes (Set, If, Code, etc.) execute normally, including credential-free I/O nodes.

tools.name: prepare_test_pin_data
tools.description: Prepare test pin data for a workflow. Returns JSON Schemas describing the expected output shape for each node that needs pin data.

tools.name: publish_workflow
tools.description: Publish (activate) a workflow to make it available for production execution. Creates an active version from the current draft.

tools.name: unpublish_workflow
tools.description: Unpublish (deactivate) a workflow to stop it from being available for production execution.

tools.name: search_projects

tools.name: search_nodes

tools.name: get_node_types

tools.name: get_suggested_nodes

tools.name: validate_workflow
tools.description: Validate n8n Workflow SDK code. Parses the code into a workflow and checks for errors. Returns the workflow JSON if valid, or detailed error messages to fix.

tools.name: create_workflow_from_code
tools.description: Create a workflow in n8n from validated SDK code. Parses the code into a workflow and saves it.

tools.name: update_workflow

tools.name: archive_workflow

tools.name: rename_data_table
tools.description: Rename an existing data table.

tools.description: Insert rows into an existing data table. Each row is an object mapping column names to values.

connectors.name: n8n (Community)
connectors.description: Community-built n8n MCP optimised for authoring workflows: in-depth knowledge of 1,650+ nodes, config validation, 2,300+ workflow templates, plus create/update/test workflows on your n8n instance.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. Open your n8n instance (Cloud, self-hosted, or community edition)
2. Go to Settings → n8n API and create a new API key (n8n version 0.183+ is required)
3. Copy the API key — you won't see it again
4. Enter your n8n base URL and the API key below — prefer an https:// URL unless you're intentionally connecting to a localhost or on-LAN n8n instance
5. Click 'Set up with Rebel' to finish

Notes:
- This is the community MCP by Romuald Czlonkowski (czlonkowski/n8n-mcp on GitHub), not the official n8n MCP server. Use it when you want richer workflow authoring: it knows 1,650+ n8n nodes in depth, validates configurations before deploying, and ships with 2,300+ workflow templates.
- The API key inherits your account's permissions, so you only get to do what your n8n account can already do.
- Requires Node.js installed on your machine (npx fetches the package on first run).
- Rebel disables this MCP's anonymous usage telemetry by default. It would otherwise send your workflow node names and your prompts' intent text to a maintainer-controlled telemetry endpoint.

tools.name: tools_documentation
tools.description: Get documentation for any tool exposed by this MCP. Start here for usage tips, parameter shapes, and recommended workflow patterns.

tools.name: search_nodes
tools.description: Full-text search across all n8n nodes (820 core + 830 community). Supports filtering by source ('community' or 'verified') and can return example configurations from real templates.

tools.name: get_node
tools.description: Get detailed information about a single n8n node. Modes: 'info' (minimal/standard/full property listing), 'docs' (human-readable markdown), 'search_properties' (find specific properties), 'versions' (compare and migrate between node versions).

tools.name: validate_node
tools.description: Validate a node configuration before adding it to a workflow. Mode 'minimal' checks required fields quickly; mode 'full' runs comprehensive validation with profiles (minimal, runtime, ai-friendly, strict) and returns actionable fixes.

tools.name: validate_workflow
tools.description: Validate a complete workflow JSON: structure, connections, expressions, and AI Agent setup. Returns errors and suggested fixes before deployment.

tools.name: search_templates
tools.description: Search the 2,300+ workflow template library. Modes: 'keyword' (text search), 'by_nodes' (templates using specific node types), 'by_task' (curated templates for common tasks), 'by_metadata' (filter by complexity, required service, target audience).

tools.name: get_template
tools.description: Get a workflow template by ID. Modes: 'nodes_only', 'structure', or 'full' workflow JSON.

tools.description: Create a new workflow on your n8n instance with nodes and connections.

tools.description: Retrieve a workflow from your n8n instance. Modes: 'full', 'details', 'structure', or 'minimal'.

tools.description: Replace an existing workflow entirely with new nodes and connections.

tools.name: n8n_update_partial_workflow
tools.description: Update a workflow using token-efficient diff operations (updateNode, addConnection, removeConnection, cleanStaleConnections, etc.). Batch multiple operations in a single call.

tools.name: n8n_delete_workflow
tools.description: Permanently delete a workflow from your n8n instance.

tools.name: n8n_list_workflows
tools.description: List workflows on your n8n instance with filtering and pagination.

tools.name: n8n_validate_workflow
tools.description: Validate a workflow that already lives on your n8n instance, by its ID.

tools.name: n8n_autofix_workflow
tools.description: Automatically fix common errors in an existing n8n workflow.

tools.name: n8n_workflow_versions
tools.description: Manage workflow version history: list versions, view a specific version, or roll back to a previous one.

tools.name: n8n_deploy_template
tools.description: Deploy a workflow template from n8n.io directly to your n8n instance, with automatic compatibility fixes.

tools.name: n8n_test_workflow
tools.description: Trigger a test execution of a workflow (webhook, form, or chat) and return the result.

tools.name: n8n_executions
tools.description: Manage workflow executions: list executions with filters, get a single execution, or delete one.

connectors.name: TalentLMS
connectors.description: TalentLMS learning management. List/create users, manage courses, enrol/unenrol learners, track progress, view groups and branches, get test and survey results, generate SSO links. 'Show all active courses' or 'Check Jane's training progress' or 'Enrol the sales team in Onboarding 101'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. Click 'Open TalentLMS' below for API setup guide
2. Log in to your TalentLMS admin panel
3. Go to Account & Settings → Security
4. Enable API access
5. Copy the API key
6. Enter your subdomain and API key below

Note: Requires a paid TalentLMS plan and Super Admin access. Rate limits: 2,000-10,000 calls/hour depending on plan.

tools.name: add_course_to_talentlms_group
tools.description:
Assign a course to a group. All group members will be enrolled.

Example: { "group_id": "5", "course_id": "10" }

tools.name: configure_talentlms
tools.description:
Configure TalentLMS API credentials. Call this when the user provides their API key and domain.

WORKFLOW:
1. Go to your TalentLMS admin panel → Account & Settings → Security
2. Enable API access
3. Copy the API key
4. Your domain is the subdomain part of your URL (e.g., "acme" for acme.talentlms.com)

Note: Requires a paid TalentLMS plan and Super Admin access.

tools.name: create_talentlms_course
tools.description:
Create a new course in TalentLMS.

Example: { "name": "Onboarding 101", "description": "New hire onboarding course" }

RELATED TOOLS:
- enrol_talentlms_user: Add users to the new course

tools.name: create_talentlms_group
tools.description:
Create a new group in TalentLMS.

Example: { "name": "Sales Team", "description": "All sales department learners" }

tools.name: create_talentlms_user
tools.description:
Create a new user in TalentLMS.

Example: { "first_name": "Jane", "last_name": "Doe", "email": "[external-email]", "login": "janedoe" }

COMMON MISTAKES:
- login must be unique across TalentLMS instance
- email must be unique unless allow_duplicate_emails is enabled in TalentLMS settings

tools.name: enrol_talentlms_user
tools.description:
Enrol a user into a course.

Example: { "user_id": "42", "course_id": "10", "role": "learner" }

COMMON MISTAKES:
- User must exist first (use create_talentlms_user if needed)
- Cannot enrol the same user twice

tools.name: get_talentlms_course
tools.description:
Get full course details by ID.

Returns: name, description, units (content structure), rules, prerequisites, certification, custom fields.

Example: { "course_id": "10" }

RELATED TOOLS:
- list_talentlms_courses: Find course IDs
- get_talentlms_course_users: See who is enrolled

tools.name: get_talentlms_course_sso_link
get_talentlms_course_users
tools.description:
Get all users enrolled in a course, with their progress and completion status.

Returns: user id, name, role, completion_status, completion_percentage, total_time.

Example: { "course_id": "10" }

RELATED TOOLS:
- get_talentlms_user_courses: See all courses for a user (reverse lookup)
- enrol_talentlms_user: Add a user to this course

tools.name: get_talentlms_group
get_talentlms_ilt_sessions
tools.description:
Get instructor-led training (ILT) sessions for a specific ILT unit.

Returns: session ID, course, instructor, date, time, location, enrolled users.

Example: { "ilt_id": "30" }

WORKFLOW:
1. Get course details with get_talentlms_course to find ILT unit IDs
2. Call this tool with the ILT unit ID

RELATED TOOLS:
- get_talentlms_course: Find ILT unit IDs from course content

tools.name: get_talentlms_site_info
get_talentlms_survey_answers
get_talentlms_test_answers
tools.description:
Get a user's answers for a specific test/quiz.

Returns: questions, user answers, correct answers, score.

Example: { "test_id": "15", "user_id": "42" }

WORKFLOW:
1. Get course details with get_talentlms_course to find test/unit IDs
2. Call this tool with the test ID and user ID

tools.name: get_talentlms_timeline
get_talentlms_user
tools.description:
Get a user's full profile by ID or email.

Returns: full profile including role, status, custom fields, last login, created date.

Example: { "user_id": "42" }
Example: { "email": "[external-email]" }

RELATED TOOLS:
- list_talentlms_users: Find user IDs
- get_talentlms_user_courses: See their enrolled courses

tools.name: get_talentlms_user_courses
tools.description:
Get all courses a user is enrolled in, with progress and completion status.

Returns: course id, name, role (learner/instructor), completion status, progress percentage, total_time, last_accessed.

Example: { "user_id": "42" }

WORKFLOW:
1. Call list_talentlms_users to find the user ID
2. Call this tool with the user ID

RELATED TOOLS:
- get_talentlms_course_users: See all users in a course (reverse lookup)

tools.name: get_talentlms_user_progress
tools.description:
Get detailed progress for a user in a specific course.

Returns: unit-by-unit progress, completion status, score, time spent per unit.

Example: { "user_id": "42", "course_id": "10" }

WORKFLOW:
1. Find user ID with list_talentlms_users
2. Find course ID with list_talentlms_courses or get_talentlms_user_courses
3. Call this tool for detailed breakdown

tools.name: list_talentlms_branches
list_talentlms_courses
tools.description:
List all courses in TalentLMS.

Returns: id, name, code, category_id, description, status, creation_date, price, creator_id.

Example: {}

RELATED TOOLS:
- get_talentlms_course: Get full course details
- get_talentlms_course_users: See enrolled users

tools.name: list_talentlms_groups
tools.description:
List all groups in TalentLMS.

Returns: id, name, description, creator_id, created_on, key (enrollment key).

Example: {}

RELATED TOOLS:
- get_talentlms_group: Get group details including members and courses

tools.name: list_talentlms_users
tools.description:
List all users in TalentLMS.

Returns: id, login, first_name, last_name, email, role, status, last_updated.

Example: {}

RELATED TOOLS:
- get_talentlms_user: Get full profile by ID
- get_talentlms_user_courses: See courses a user is enrolled in

tools.name: set_talentlms_user_status
tools.description:
Activate or deactivate a user in TalentLMS.

Example: { "user_id": "42", "status": "active" }

RELATED TOOLS:
- list_talentlms_users: Find user IDs

tools.name: unenrol_talentlms_user
tools.description:
Remove a user from a course.

Example: { "user_id": "42", "course_id": "10" }

RELATED TOOLS:
- get_talentlms_course_users: Check current enrolment

connectors.name: Zoom
connectors.description: Zoom meetings: list upcoming/past meetings, schedule new meetings, delete meetings, get meeting details. 'Show my Zoom meetings' or 'Schedule a Zoom call tomorrow at 2pm'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. Click 'Open Zoom' below to go to the Zoom Marketplace
2. Click 'Develop' → 'Build App'
3. Choose 'Server-to-Server OAuth' app type
4. Fill in the app name (e.g., 'Rebel AI')
5. Copy the Account ID, Client ID, and Client Secret from the App Credentials page
6. Add scopes: Meeting → all meeting permissions
7. Activate the app
8. Paste your credentials below

Note: Requires a Zoom account with app development access. Server-to-Server OAuth does not require end-user login.

tools.name: create_meeting
tools.description: Create a meeting

tools.name: delete_a_meeting
tools.description: Delete a meeting with a given ID

tools.name: get_a_meeting_details
tools.description: Retrieve the meeting's details with a given ID

tools.name: list_meetings
tools.description: List scheduled meetings

connectors.name: BambooHR
connectors.description: BambooHR HR platform. Employee directory, time-off requests and balances, goals and performance, recruiting (ATS), benefits, time tracking, certifications, reports. 'Who's out today?' or 'Show the employee directory' or 'What's my PTO balance?'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. Log in to your BambooHR account
2. Click your name in the lower-left corner
3. Select 'API Keys'
4. Click 'Add New Key' and name it (e.g., 'Rebel')
5. Copy the key immediately — it won't be shown again
6. Enter your company subdomain and API key below

Your subdomain is the part before .bamboohr.com in your URL (e.g., 'acme' for acme.bamboohr.com).

Note: The API key inherits your account permissions. Requires Node.js installed locally.

tools.name: bamboohr_add_application_comment
tools.description:
Add a comment/note to a job application.

Posts feedback, interview notes, or other comments to an application.

Args:
  - application_id (string): Application ID (required)
  - comment (string): Comment text (required)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation.

Examples:
  - "Add interview feedback to application 123"
  - "Note that candidate 456 needs follow-up"

tools.name: bamboohr_add_employee_dependent
tools.description:
Add a dependent/family member to an employee's record.

Records a new dependent for benefits enrollment.

Args:
  - employee_id (string): Employee ID (required)
  - first_name (string): Dependent's first name (required)
  - last_name (string): Dependent's last name (required)
  - relationship (string): Relationship to employee - 'spouse', 'child', 'domestic partner', etc. (required)
  - date_of_birth (string): Date of birth (YYYY-MM-DD) (required)
  - gender (string): Gender - 'male', 'female' (optional)
  - ssn (string): Social Security Number (optional, for US employees)
  - address_same_as_employee (boolean): Whether dependent has same address (optional, defaults to true)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation with dependent ID.

Examples:
  - "Add spouse for employee 123"
  - "Record a new child dependent for employee 456"

tools.name: bamboohr_add_employee_table_row
tools.description:
Add a new row to an employee table (e.g., job history, compensation, employment status).

Adds historical data like a new job title change, compensation update, or employment status change.

Common table names:
  - jobInfo: Job title, department, location, supervisor changes
  - compensation: Pay rate, pay type, pay frequency changes
  - employmentStatus: Status changes (active, terminated, leave, etc.)
  - customTable: Custom table data

Args:
  - employee_id (string): Employee ID (required)
  - table_name (string): Table name to add row to (required)
  - row_data (object): Row data as key-value pairs (required)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Example row_data for jobInfo:
  {"date": "2024-01-01", "jobTitle": "Senior Engineer", "department": "Engineering"}

Example row_data for compensation:
  {"date": "2024-01-01", "rate": "85000", "type": "Salary", "frequency": "Annual"}

Returns:
  Success confirmation with new row ID.

Examples:
  - "Add a promotion to Senior Engineer for employee 123 effective Jan 1"
  - "Record a salary increase for employee 456"

tools.name: bamboohr_add_goal_comment
tools.description:
Add a comment or progress update to a goal.

Posts a comment to track progress, provide feedback, or document updates.

Args:
  - employee_id (string): Employee ID (required)
  - goal_id (string): Goal ID (required)
  - comment (string): Comment text (required)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation.

Examples:
  - "Add a progress update to goal 456"
  - "Comment on employee 123's certification goal"

tools.name: bamboohr_add_hour_record
tools.description:
Add an hour record for an employee.

Manually records hours worked for hourly employees.

Args:
  - employee_id (string): Employee ID (required)
  - date (string): Date hours worked (YYYY-MM-DD) (required)
  - hours (number): Number of hours worked (required)
  - project_id (string): Project ID for time allocation (optional)
  - task_id (string): Task ID for time allocation (optional)
  - note (string): Note about the hours (optional)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation with record ID.

Examples:
  - "Add 8 hours for employee 123 on January 15"
  - "Record 4 hours of overtime for employee 456"

tools.name: bamboohr_add_training_record
tools.description:
Add a training completion record for an employee.

Records that an employee has completed a training course or certification.

Args:
  - employee_id (string): Employee ID (required)
  - training_type_id (string): Training type ID (use bamboohr_get_training_types to see options) (required)
  - completed_date (string): Date training was completed (YYYY-MM-DD) (required)
  - notes (string): Notes about the training (optional)
  - cost (number): Cost of the training (optional)
  - credits (number): Credits/CEUs earned (optional)
  - hours (number): Hours of training (optional)
  - instructor (string): Instructor name (optional)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation with record ID.

Examples:
  - "Record that employee 123 completed safety training on Jan 15"
  - "Add AWS certification for employee 456"

tools.name: bamboohr_adjust_time_off_balance
tools.description:
Adjust an employee's time off balance (add or subtract hours/days).

Manually adjust PTO balances for corrections, special grants, or deductions.

**Safety Note:** Negative adjustments (reducing balance) require careful consideration.

Args:
  - employee_id (string): Employee ID (required)
  - time_off_type_id (string): Time off type ID to adjust (required)
  - adjustment_amount (number): Amount to adjust (positive to add, negative to subtract) (required)
  - effective_date (string): Date the adjustment takes effect (YYYY-MM-DD) (required)
  - note (string): Reason for the adjustment (required)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation with new balance.

Examples:
  - "Add 8 hours of vacation for employee 123 as a bonus"
  - "Deduct 4 hours of sick time that was incorrectly added"

Error Handling:
  - Returns "Access forbidden" if user lacks permission (403)
  - Returns validation error if balance would go negative

tools.name: bamboohr_assign_time_off_policy
tools.description:
Assign a time off policy to an employee.

Associates an employee with a specific time off policy for accrual tracking.

Args:
  - employee_id (string): Employee ID (required)
  - time_off_type_id (string): Time off type ID (required)
  - accrual_start_date (string): Date accrual begins (YYYY-MM-DD) (required)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation.

Examples:
  - "Assign the standard vacation policy to new employee 123"
  - "Add sick time policy starting from hire date"

Error Handling:
  - Returns "Access forbidden" if user lacks permission (403)
  - Returns error if policy doesn't exist

tools.name: bamboohr_change_time_off_request_status
tools.description:
Approve, deny, or cancel a time off request.

Allows managers to approve or deny time off requests, or employees to cancel their own requests.

**Safety Note:** When denying a request, you must provide a note explaining the reason.

Args:
  - request_id (string): Time off request ID (required)
  - status (string): New status - 'approved', 'denied', or 'canceled' (required)
  - note (string): Note explaining the decision (required when denying, optional otherwise)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation.

Examples:
  - "Approve time off request 456"
  - "Deny vacation request with note 'Insufficient coverage'"
  - "Cancel my time off request 789"

Error Handling:
  - Returns "Resource not found" if request doesn't exist (404)
  - Returns "Access forbidden" if user lacks permission to change status (403)

tools.name: bamboohr_clock_in
tools.description:
Clock in an employee for time tracking.

Records the start of a work period for an employee.

Args:
  - employee_id (string): Employee ID (required)
  - timestamp (string): Clock-in timestamp in ISO 8601 format (optional, defaults to now)
  - timezone (string): Timezone (e.g., 'America/New_York') (optional)
  - note (string): Optional note (optional)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation with clock-in time.

Examples:
  - "Clock in employee 123"
  - "Record clock in for employee 456 at 9am"

tools.name: bamboohr_clock_out
tools.description:
Clock out an employee for time tracking.

Records the end of a work period for an employee.

Args:
  - employee_id (string): Employee ID (required)
  - timestamp (string): Clock-out timestamp in ISO 8601 format (optional, defaults to now)
  - timezone (string): Timezone (optional)
  - note (string): Optional note (optional)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation with clock-out time.

Examples:
  - "Clock out employee 123"
  - "Record clock out for employee 456 at 5pm"

tools.name: bamboohr_create_candidate
tools.description:
Create a new candidate application for a job.

Adds a new candidate to the ATS for a specific job opening.

Args:
  - job_id (string): Job ID to apply for (required)
  - first_name (string): Candidate first name (required)
  - last_name (string): Candidate last name (required)
  - email (string): Candidate email (required)
  - phone (string): Candidate phone number (optional)
  - source (string): Application source (e.g., "LinkedIn", "Referral", "Website") (optional)
  - resume_text (string): Resume content as text (optional)
  - cover_letter (string): Cover letter content (optional)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Created application with ID.

Examples:
  - "Add candidate John Smith to job 123"
  - "Create an application for the developer position"

tools.name: bamboohr_create_employee
tools.description:
Create a new employee record in BambooHR.

Creates a new employee with the provided information. Required fields typically include firstName and lastName at minimum.

Args:
  - first_name (string): Employee's first name (required)
  - last_name (string): Employee's last name (required)
  - email (string): Employee's email address (optional)
  - hire_date (string): Hire date in YYYY-MM-DD format (optional)
  - job_title (string): Job title (optional)
  - department (string): Department name (optional)
  - location (string): Work location (optional)
  - additional_fields (object): Any additional fields to set (optional)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Created employee information including the new employee ID.

Examples:
  - "Add a new employee named Jane Smith"
  - "Create employee record for new hire starting January 15th"

Error Handling:
  - Returns validation errors if required fields are missing
  - Returns "Access forbidden" if user lacks permission to create employees (403)

tools.name: bamboohr_create_goal
tools.description:
Create a new goal for an employee.

Creates a goal with specified details including title, description, and due date.

Args:
  - employee_id (string): Employee ID (required)
  - title (string): Goal title (required)
  - description (string): Goal description (optional)
  - due_date (string): Due date in YYYY-MM-DD format (optional)
  - percent_complete (number): Initial progress percentage 0-100 (optional, defaults to 0)
  - shared_with_employee (boolean): Whether employee can see this goal (optional, defaults to true)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Created goal with ID.

Examples:
  - "Create a goal for employee 123 to complete certification by Q2"
  - "Add a professional development goal for employee 456"

tools.name: bamboohr_create_job_opening
tools.description:
Create a new job opening/posting.

Creates a new job requisition in the ATS.

Args:
  - title (string): Job title (required)
  - department_id (string): Department ID (optional)
  - location_id (string): Location ID (optional)
  - description (string): Job description (optional)
  - minimum_experience (string): Minimum experience required (optional)
  - employment_status_id (string): Employment status ID (full-time, part-time, etc.) (optional)
  - hiring_lead_id (string): Employee ID of hiring lead (optional)
  - internal_job_code (string): Internal job code (optional)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Created job with ID.

Examples:
  - "Create a job opening for Senior Developer"
  - "Post a new Marketing Manager position"

tools.name: bamboohr_create_time_off_request
tools.description:
Create a new time off request for an employee.

Submits a time off request with specified details. The request can be marked as approved immediately
if the user has appropriate permissions.

Args:
  - employee_id (string): Employee ID for the request (required)
  - start_date (string): Start date (YYYY-MM-DD format) (required)
  - end_date (string): End date (YYYY-MM-DD format) (required)
  - time_off_type_id (string): Time off type ID (use bamboohr_get_time_off_types to see available types) (required)
  - amount (number): Amount of time off in days or hours depending on policy (required)
  - status (string): 'approved' or 'requested' (optional, defaults to 'requested')
  - notes (string): Additional notes for the request (optional)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Created time off request details including request ID.

Examples:
  - "Request vacation for employee 123 from Dec 20-25"
  - "Create sick leave request for tomorrow"

Error Handling:
  - Returns validation errors if dates are invalid or overlap existing requests
  - Returns "Access forbidden" if user lacks permission (403)

tools.name: bamboohr_delete_employee_file
tools.description:
Delete a file from an employee's profile.

**⚠️ DESTRUCTIVE OPERATION:** This permanently removes the file. Requires explicit confirmation.

Args:
  - employee_id (string): Employee ID (required)
  - file_id (string): File ID to delete (required)
  - confirm (boolean): Must be set to true to confirm deletion (required)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation.

Examples:
  - "Delete file 789 from employee 123's profile"

Error Handling:
  - Returns error if confirm is not true
  - Returns "Resource not found" if file doesn't exist (404)
  - Returns "Access forbidden" if user lacks permission (403)

tools.name: bamboohr_delete_goal
tools.description:
Delete a goal from an employee's record.

**⚠️ DESTRUCTIVE OPERATION:** This permanently removes the goal. Requires explicit confirmation.

Args:
  - employee_id (string): Employee ID (required)
  - goal_id (string): Goal ID to delete (required)
  - confirm (boolean): Must be set to true to confirm deletion (required)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation.

Examples:
  - "Delete goal 789 for employee 123"

Error Handling:
  - Returns error if confirm is not true
  - Returns "Resource not found" if goal doesn't exist (404)
  - Returns "Access forbidden" if user lacks permission (403)

tools.name: bamboohr_delete_training_record
tools.description:
Delete a training record.

**⚠️ DESTRUCTIVE OPERATION:** This permanently removes the training record. Requires explicit confirmation.

Args:
  - record_id (string): Training record ID to delete (required)
  - confirm (boolean): Must be set to true to confirm deletion (required)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation.

Error Handling:
  - Returns error if confirm is not true
  - Returns "Resource not found" if record doesn't exist (404)

tools.name: bamboohr_get_certifications_due

  - status ('active'|'expiring_soon'|'expired'|'not_started'|'all'): Filter by status
  - employee_id (string): Filter by specific employee
  - response_format ('json'|'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:

Examples:

  - "List expired certifications for employee 123"

tools.name: bamboohr_get_dataset_fields

Returns:
  List of fields with ID, name, type, and entity information.

Field Types and Operators:
  - text: contains, does_not_contain, equal, not_equal, empty, not_empty
  - date: lt, lte, gt, gte, last, next, range, equal, not_equal, empty, not_empty
  - int: equal, not_equal, gte, gt, lte, lt, empty, not_empty
  - bool: checked, not_checked
  - options: includes, does_not_include, empty, not_empty

tools.name: bamboohr_query_dataset
tools.description:
Query data from a BambooHR dataset with filters, sorting, and aggregations.

Powerful API for retrieving filtered, sorted, and aggregated employee data.
Use bamboohr_get_dataset_fields to discover available fields first.

Args:
  - dataset_name (string): Dataset name (required)
  - fields (string[]): Fields to include in results (required)
  - filters (array): Filter conditions with {field, operator, value}
  - sort_by (array): Sort conditions with {field, sort: 'asc'|'desc'}
  - group_by (string[]): Fields to group by (one field supported)
  - aggregations (array): Aggregations with {field, type}
  - show_history (string[]): Entity names to include history for
  - limit (number): Max results (default: 20)
  - offset (number): Skip results for pagination
  - response_format ('json'|'markdown'): Output format

Filter Operators by Type:
  - text: contains, does_not_contain, equal, not_equal, empty, not_empty
  - date: lt, lte, gt, gte, last, next, range, equal, not_equal
  - int: equal, not_equal, gte, gt, lte, lt
  - bool: checked, not_checked
  - options: includes, does_not_include (use arrays: ["value1", "value2"])

Special Filter Values:
  - last/next: {"duration": "5", "unit": "years"} (days, weeks, months, years)
  - range: {"start": "2023-01-01", "end": "2023-12-31"}

Examples:
  - "Get all active employees with their department and job title"
  - "Find employees hired in the last 6 months"
  - "Count employees by department"

tools.name: bamboohr_request_custom_report
tools.description:
Generate an ad-hoc custom report with specified fields.

Creates a one-time report by specifying which fields to include.
Different from bamboohr_run_report which executes saved reports.

Note: This endpoint is deprecated by BambooHR in favor of the Datasets API.
Use bamboohr_query_dataset for new implementations.

Args:
  - title (string): Optional report title
  - fields (string[]): Field IDs/aliases to include (required)
  - filters (object): Optional field:value filter pairs
  - format ('JSON'|'CSV'|'XML'|'PDF'|'XLS'): Output format
  - response_format ('json'|'markdown'): MCP output format

Returns:
  Report data with requested fields for all matching employees.

Examples:
  - "Generate a report with firstName, lastName, and hireDate"
  - "Create an ad-hoc report of employee contact info"

tools.name: bamboohr_run_report
tools.description:
Execute a saved report by ID and return the data.

Runs a report created in BambooHR's Reports section. Get report IDs from
bamboohr_list_reports. Supports multiple output formats.

Args:
  - report_id (string): Report ID from bamboohr_list_reports (required)
  - format ('json'|'csv'|'xml'|'pdf'|'xls'): Data format (default: 'json')
  - response_format ('json'|'markdown'): Output format

Returns:
  Report data in the requested format.

Examples:
  - "Run the Feedback Status report"
  - "Execute report 123 as CSV"
  - "Get the data from report ID 456"

tools.name: bamboohr_update_applicant_status
tools.description:
Update the status of a job application.

Moves an application through the hiring pipeline (e.g., screen → interview → offer).

Args:
  - application_id (string): Application ID (required)
  - status_id (string): New status ID (use bamboohr_get_application_statuses to see options) (required)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation.

Examples:
  - "Move application 123 to interview stage"
  - "Mark application 456 as hired"

tools.name: bamboohr_update_employee
tools.description:
Update employee information for a specific employee.

Allows updating various employee fields including personal information, job details, and custom fields.
Only updates the fields provided - existing fields not included will remain unchanged.

Args:
  - employee_id (string): Employee ID to update (required)
  - update_data (object): Fields to update with their new values (required)
    Example: {"firstName": "John", "lastName": "Doe", "email": "john@example.com", "customField123": "value"}
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Common updatable fields:
  - firstName, lastName, email
  - jobTitle, department, location
  - supervisor, hireDate, status
  - Any custom fields by their field name/ID

Returns:
  Success confirmation or error message.

Examples:
  - "Update employee 123's email to [external-email]"
  - "Change John's job title to Senior Developer"
  - "Update custom field 'Skills' for employee 456"

Error Handling:
  - Returns "Resource not found" if employee doesn't exist (404)
  - Returns "Access forbidden" if user lacks permission to update (403)
  - Returns field-specific errors if validation fails

tools.name: bamboohr_update_employee_dependent
tools.description:
Update information for an employee's dependent.

Modifies dependent details like name, relationship, or contact information.

Args:
  - employee_id (string): Employee ID (required)
  - dependent_id (string): Dependent ID to update (required)
  - first_name (string): Updated first name (optional)
  - last_name (string): Updated last name (optional)
  - relationship (string): Updated relationship (optional)
  - date_of_birth (string): Updated date of birth (YYYY-MM-DD) (optional)
  - gender (string): Updated gender (optional)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation.

Examples:
  - "Update dependent 789's last name for employee 123"
  - "Correct date of birth for dependent 456"

tools.name: bamboohr_update_employee_table_row
tools.description:
Update an existing row in an employee table.

Modifies an existing historical record like a job info entry, compensation record, or employment status.

Args:
  - employee_id (string): Employee ID (required)
  - table_name (string): Table name containing the row (required)
  - row_id (string): ID of the row to update (required)
  - row_data (object): Fields to update with new values (required)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation.

Examples:
  - "Correct the effective date on employee 123's last promotion"
  - "Fix the salary amount in compensation record 789"

tools.name: bamboohr_update_goal
tools.description:
Update an existing goal.

Modifies goal details like title, description, progress, or status.

Args:
  - employee_id (string): Employee ID (required)
  - goal_id (string): Goal ID to update (required)
  - title (string): New title (optional)
  - description (string): New description (optional)
  - due_date (string): New due date (YYYY-MM-DD) (optional)
  - percent_complete (number): Progress percentage 0-100 (optional)
  - status (string): Goal status - 'in_progress', 'completed', 'on_hold' (optional)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation.

Examples:
  - "Update goal 789 progress to 50%"
  - "Mark goal 456 as completed"
  - "Change the due date for goal 123"

tools.name: bamboohr_update_hour_record
tools.description:
Update an existing hour record.

Modifies hours or notes for an existing time entry.

Args:
  - record_id (string): Hour record ID to update (required)
  - hours (number): Updated hours (optional)
  - note (string): Updated note (optional)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation.

Examples:
  - "Change record 789 to 7.5 hours"
  - "Add a note to hour record 456"

tools.name: bamboohr_update_list_field_values
tools.description:
Update options for a list/dropdown field.

Adds, updates, or archives options for dropdown fields like department, location, etc.

Args:
  - list_field_id (string): List field ID to update (required)
  - options (array): Array of option objects with value and optional id for updates (required)
    - For new options: { "value": "New Option Name" }
    - For updates: { "id": "123", "value": "Updated Name" }
    - For archiving: { "id": "123", "archived": true }
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation.

Examples:
  - "Add a new department called 'Product'"
  - "Update location 123 to 'New York Office'"
  - "Archive the old department option"

tools.name: bamboohr_update_training_record
tools.description:
Update an existing training record.

Modifies details of a training completion record.

Args:
  - record_id (string): Training record ID to update (required)
  - completed_date (string): New completion date (YYYY-MM-DD) (optional)
  - notes (string): Updated notes (optional)
  - cost (number): Updated cost (optional)
  - credits (number): Updated credits (optional)
  - hours (number): Updated hours (optional)
  - instructor (string): Updated instructor (optional)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation.

Examples:
  - "Update the completion date for training record 789"
  - "Add notes to training record 456"

tools.name: bamboohr_upload_company_file
tools.description:
Upload a file to the company files section.

Uploads a document to company-wide file storage.

Args:
  - file_name (string): Name of the file including extension (required)
  - file_data (string): Base64-encoded file content (required)
  - folder_id (string): Folder ID to upload to (optional, defaults to root)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation with file ID.

Examples:
  - "Upload the company handbook PDF"
  - "Add the benefits guide to company files"

Error Handling:
  - Returns "Access forbidden" if user lacks permission (403)

tools.name: bamboohr_upload_employee_file
tools.description:
Upload a file to an employee's profile.

Uploads a document (PDF, DOC, DOCX, XLS, XLSX, PNG, JPG, TXT, etc.) to an employee's file folder.

Args:
  - employee_id (string): Employee ID (required)
  - file_name (string): Name of the file including extension (required)
  - file_data (string): Base64-encoded file content (required)
  - category_id (string): File category ID (use bamboohr_get_file_categories to see options) (optional)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation with file ID.

Examples:
  - "Upload employee 123's signed offer letter"
  - "Add a PDF certification to employee 456's file"

Error Handling:
  - Returns "Access forbidden" if user lacks permission (403)
  - Returns validation error if file type not allowed

tools.name: bamboohr_upload_employee_photo
tools.description:
Upload a profile photo for an employee.

Uploads a photo (JPEG or PNG) for an employee's profile picture.

Args:
  - employee_id (string): Employee ID (required)
  - photo_data (string): Base64-encoded photo data (JPEG or PNG) (required)
  - response_format ('json' | 'markdown'): Output format (default: 'markdown')

Returns:
  Success confirmation.

Examples:
  - "Upload a new profile photo for employee 123"

Error Handling:
  - Returns "Access forbidden" if user lacks permission (403)
  - Returns validation error if image format not supported

Note: Google Maps API usage is billed per request. New accounts get $200/month free credit. See https://developers.google.com/maps/billing-and-pricing for pricing details.

connectors.name: MoEngage
connectors.description: Campaign analytics: email, push, SMS campaign stats, delivery rates, CTR, and search. 'Show my active email campaigns' or 'Get campaign stats for the last 30 days'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. Click 'Open MoEngage' below to go to your Dashboard
2. Navigate to Settings > Account > APIs
3. Copy your Workspace ID and Campaign Reports API Key
4. Check your dashboard URL for data center (e.g., dashboard-02.moengage.com = DC02)
5. Enter the details below

Requires: Python 3.10+ and uv (get uv from https://astral.sh/uv)

Note: API key must have Campaign Reports permissions.

tools.name: get_campaign_stats
tools.description:
Fetch campaign statistics from MoEngage Stats API.

Retrieves performance metrics for specified campaigns within a date range.
Stats include delivery metrics (sent, failed), engagement metrics
(impressions, clicks, CTR), and rate calculations.

Args:
    campaign_ids: List of campaign IDs to fetch stats for (max 50).
        Example: ["6643773d4fe37fe7a10b718e", "6645fbae1101e4c6611b15c7"]
    start_date: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format.
        Example: "2024-12-01"
    end_date: End date in YYYY-MM-DD format.
        Example: "2024-12-31"
    attribution_type: Attribution model - "VIEW_THROUGH" or "CLICK_THROUGH".
        Defaults to "VIEW_THROUGH".
    metric_type: Metric aggregation - "TOTAL" or "UNIQUE".
        Defaults to "TOTAL".
    request_id: Optional request ID for tracking.
        Defaults to "mcp-request".
    workspace_id: MoEngage Workspace ID (required in multi-tenant mode).
    campaign_reports_api_key: MoEngage Campaign Reports API Key (required in multi-tenant mode).
    data_center: MoEngage Data Center (01-06). Defaults to "01".
    ctx: MCP context (injected automatically).

Returns:
    StatsResponse with campaign statistics including:
    - success: Whether the request succeeded
    - campaigns: List of campaign stats with metrics
    - error_message: Error details if request failed
    - rate_limit_remaining: Remaining API calls this minute

Raises:
    ValueError: If parameters are invalid
    MoEngageAPIError: If API request fails

Example:
    >>> result = await get_campaign_stats(
    ...     campaign_ids=["abc123", "def456"],
    ...     start_date="2024-12-01",
    ...     end_date="2024-12-31"
    ... )
    >>> for campaign in result.campaigns:
    ...     print(f"{campaign.campaign_id}: {campaign.all_variations.sent} sent")

tools.name: get_email_campaigns
tools.description:
Get Email campaign details from MoEngage.

Retrieves a list of Email campaigns with their configuration,
content, and metadata.

Args:
    campaign_id: Optional specific campaign ID to fetch.
    campaign_name: Optional campaign name to filter by.
    status: Optional filter by status (DRAFT, SCHEDULED, ACTIVE, PAUSED, COMPLETED, STOPPED).
    limit: Number of campaigns to return per page (1-15, default 10).
    page: Page number for pagination (default 1).
    include_archived: Include archived campaigns (default False).
    workspace_id: MoEngage Workspace ID (required in multi-tenant mode).
    campaign_reports_api_key: MoEngage Campaign Reports API Key (required in multi-tenant mode).
    data_center: MoEngage Data Center (01-06). Defaults to "01".
    ctx: MCP context (injected automatically).

Returns:
    CampaignSearchResponse with Email campaign details including:
    - campaign_id, channel, status, delivery_type
    - basic_details (name, team, tags)
    - variations (subject, from_name, from_email, etc.)
    - utm_params, connector info

tools.name: get_push_campaigns
tools.description:
Get Push notification campaign details from MoEngage.

Retrieves a list of Push campaigns with their configuration,
content, and metadata.

Args:
    campaign_id: Optional specific campaign ID to fetch.
    campaign_name: Optional campaign name to filter by.
    status: Optional filter by status (DRAFT, SCHEDULED, ACTIVE, PAUSED, COMPLETED, STOPPED).
    limit: Number of campaigns to return per page (1-15, default 10).
    page: Page number for pagination (default 1).
    include_archived: Include archived campaigns (default False).
    workspace_id: MoEngage Workspace ID (required in multi-tenant mode).
    campaign_reports_api_key: MoEngage Campaign Reports API Key (required in multi-tenant mode).
    data_center: MoEngage Data Center (01-06). Defaults to "01".
    ctx: MCP context (injected automatically).

Returns:
    CampaignSearchResponse with Push campaign details including:
    - campaign_id, channel, platform, status, delivery_type
    - basic_details (name, team, tags)
    - variations (title, message, etc.)
    - utm_params, connector info

tools.name: get_sms_campaigns
tools.description:
Get SMS campaign details from MoEngage.

Retrieves a list of SMS campaigns with their configuration,
content, and metadata.

Args:
    campaign_id: Optional specific campaign ID to fetch.
    campaign_name: Optional campaign name to filter by.
    status: Optional filter by status (DRAFT, SCHEDULED, ACTIVE, PAUSED, COMPLETED, STOPPED).
    limit: Number of campaigns to return per page (1-15, default 10).
    page: Page number for pagination (default 1).
    include_archived: Include archived campaigns (default False).
    workspace_id: MoEngage Workspace ID (required in multi-tenant mode).
    campaign_reports_api_key: MoEngage Campaign Reports API Key (required in multi-tenant mode).
    data_center: MoEngage Data Center (01-06). Defaults to "01".
    ctx: MCP context (injected automatically).

Returns:
    CampaignSearchResponse with SMS campaign details including:
    - campaign_id, channel, status, delivery_type
    - basic_details (name, team, tags)
    - variations (message content, etc.)
    - connector info (SMS provider)

connectors.name: Playwright
connectors.description: Official Microsoft Playwright browser automation. Navigate pages, click elements, fill forms, take screenshots, extract text via accessibility snapshots. Self-contained — no browser extension needed. Use ONLY when explicitly asked to automate a browser. 'Navigate to this page and fill the form' or 'Take a screenshot of example.com'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
Playwright MCP runs locally — no API key or account needed.

On first use, Playwright will download browser binaries (~200MB). This happens once and may take a minute.

A browser window will open when Rebel uses browser tools. Close it when done.

Requires Node.js 18+ installed locally.

connectors.name: Brave Search
connectors.description: Brave's independent web search index with support for web, images, videos, news, local places, and AI summaries. A reliable alternative when built-in search is rate-limited. 'Search Brave for privacy-respecting news aggregators' or 'Get local coffee shops near Shoreditch'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. Click 'Open Brave' below to go to API Keys
2. Create a new API key (or copy an existing one)
3. Paste the key here

Note: Free tier includes 2,000 queries/month. Paid plans available for higher volume.

capabilities.promptGuidance: Brave Search provides an independent web search index via MCP, complementing the built-in WebSearch tool. Use `brave_local_search` for business/place/POI lookups, `brave_image_search` / `brave_video_search` / `brave_news_search` for media-specific results, and `brave_summarizer` for AI-synthesized answers with inline references. Fall back to `brave_web_search` for general web queries when the built-in WebSearch is unavailable or returns low-quality results.

tools.name: brave_web_search
tools.description: Search the web using Brave Search. Returns ranked web results with titles, URLs, snippets, and page ages. Best for: general web queries, news articles, reference lookups. Supports country/language filters, freshness filters (pd/pw/pm/py), and result type filters. For place/POI lookups, use brave_local_search. For AI-synthesised answers, use brave_summarizer.

tools.name: brave_local_search
tools.description: Search for local businesses, points of interest, and places using Brave's local index. Returns business names, addresses, phone numbers, opening hours, ratings, and map links. Best for: restaurants, shops, services, venues. Requires a textual location (city/neighbourhood) or explicit lat/lng.

tools.name: brave_image_search
tools.description: Search the web for images. Returns image URLs, source pages, dimensions, and format. Best for: finding illustrative photos, diagrams, product images. Supports safesearch, size, and colour filters.

tools.name: brave_video_search
tools.description: Search the web for videos. Returns video URLs (YouTube, Vimeo, etc.), titles, durations, upload dates, and thumbnails. Best for: finding talks, tutorials, news clips.

tools.name: brave_news_search
tools.description: Search news articles using Brave's news index. Returns headlines, publishers, URLs, and publication timestamps. Supports freshness filters (past day/week/month). Best for: current events, press coverage, recent announcements.

tools.name: brave_summarizer
tools.description: Get an AI-generated summary of search results with inline references. Returns a synthesized text answer with numbered citations linking back to source URLs. Best for: quick factual answers, topic overviews. Slower than brave_web_search. For raw result lists, use brave_web_search instead.

connectors.name: Perplexity
connectors.description: AI-powered web search, deep research, and reasoning. Search the web, ask complex questions, conduct multi-step research, and get cited answers. 'Search for recent news about AI regulation' or 'Research the competitive landscape for project management tools'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. Click 'Open Perplexity' below to go to API settings
2. Generate an API key (or copy existing one)
3. Copy the key
4. Paste the key here

Note: Requires a Perplexity API subscription. Free tier includes limited searches.

capabilities.promptGuidance: Perplexity provides AI-powered web search via MCP. Use `perplexity_search` for quick web searches, `perplexity_ask` for questions requiring synthesis, `perplexity_research` for multi-step deep research, and `perplexity_reason` for complex analytical questions. These tools provide cited, up-to-date answers superior to built-in web search.

tools.name: perplexity_ask
tools.description: Answer a question using web-grounded AI (Sonar Pro model). Best for: quick factual questions, summaries, explanations, and general Q&A. Returns a text response with numbered citations. Fastest and cheapest option. Supports filtering by recency (hour/day/week/month/year), domain restrictions, and search context size. For in-depth multi-source research, use perplexity_research instead. For step-by-step reasoning and analysis, use perplexity_reason instead.

tools.name: perplexity_reason
tools.description: Analyze a question using step-by-step reasoning with web grounding (Sonar Reasoning Pro model). Best for: math, logic, comparisons, complex arguments, and tasks requiring chain-of-thought. Returns a reasoned response with numbered citations. Supports filtering by recency (hour/day/week/month/year), domain restrictions, and search context size. For quick factual questions, use perplexity_ask instead. For comprehensive multi-source research, use perplexity_research instead.

tools.name: perplexity_research
tools.description: Conduct deep, multi-source research on a topic (Sonar Deep Research model). Best for: literature reviews, comprehensive overviews, investigative queries needing many sources. Returns a detailed response with numbered citations. Significantly slower than other tools (30+ seconds). For quick factual questions, use perplexity_ask instead. For logical analysis and reasoning, use perplexity_reason instead.

tools.name: perplexity_search
tools.description: Search the web and return a ranked list of results with titles, URLs, snippets, and dates. Best for: finding specific URLs, checking recent news, verifying facts, discovering sources. Returns formatted results (title, URL, snippet, date) — no AI synthesis. For AI-generated answers with citations, use perplexity_ask instead.

connectors.name: Tavily
connectors.description: Agent-optimized web search, content extraction, site crawling, and site mapping. Designed for AI agents with structured, relevant results. 'Search for the latest pricing of Notion' or 'Extract the main content from this URL'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. Click 'Open Tavily' below to go to the dashboard
2. Copy your API key from the dashboard
3. Paste the key here

Note: Free tier includes 1,000 searches/month. Paid plans available for higher volume.

capabilities.promptGuidance: Tavily provides agent-optimized web search via MCP. Use `tavily-search` for web searches with structured results, `tavily-extract` to get clean content from URLs, `tavily-crawl` to crawl a site, and `tavily-map` to get a site's URL structure. These tools return AI-optimized results with relevance scoring.

tools.name: tavily_crawl
tools.description: Crawl a website starting from a URL. Extracts content from pages with configurable depth and breadth.

tools.name: tavily_extract
tools.description: Extract content from URLs. Returns raw page content in markdown or text format.

tools.name: tavily_map
tools.description: Map a website's structure. Returns a list of URLs found starting from the base URL.

tools.name: tavily_research
tools.description: Perform comprehensive research on a given topic or question. Use this tool when you need to gather information from multiple sources to answer a question or complete a task. Returns a detailed response based on the research findings. Rate limit: 20 requests per minute.

tools.name: tavily_search
tools.description: Search the web for current information on any topic. Use for news, facts, or data beyond your knowledge cutoff. Returns snippets and source URLs.

connectors.name: Exa
connectors.description: Neural/semantic web search, code search, and company research. Find content by meaning, not just keywords. 'Find academic papers about transformer architectures' or 'Research companies building AI developer tools'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. Click 'Open Exa' below to go to API Keys page
2. Create a new API key (or copy existing one)
3. Paste the key here

Note: Free tier includes 1,000 searches/month.

capabilities.promptGuidance: Exa provides neural/semantic web search via MCP. Use Exa's search tools for finding content by meaning (semantic search), code search, academic papers, and company research. Exa excels at finding specific types of content and understanding search intent beyond keywords.

tools.name: get_code_context_exa
tools.description:
Find code examples, documentation, and programming solutions. Searches GitHub, Stack Overflow, and official docs.

Best for: Any programming question - API usage, library examples, code snippets, debugging help.
Returns: Relevant code and documentation, formatted for easy reading.

tools.name: web_search_exa
tools.description:
Search the web for any topic and get clean, ready-to-use content.

Best for: Finding current information, news, facts, or answering questions about any topic.
Returns: Clean text content from top search results, ready for LLM use.

connectors.name: Smartsheet
connectors.description: Smartsheet work management: browse workspaces, read and update sheets, manage rows and columns, run reports and dashboards, handle attachments and comments. 'What's on my project sheet?' or 'Add a row to the launch tracker' or 'Summarise the Q2 roadmap sheet'.
connectors.setupInstructions:
1. Click 'Open Smartsheet' below and sign in if needed
2. Go to Account > Personal Settings > API Access
3. Click 'Generate new access token', name it (e.g., 'Rebel'), and copy the token
4. Paste the token below
5. Pick your region (leave on US unless your Smartsheet account is on EU or AU)

Requires a Business, Enterprise, or Advanced Work Management plan. The token inherits your Smartsheet permissions.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Search across accessible sheets, reports, dashboards, folders, workspaces, cell data, comments, and attachments.

tools.name: browse_workspace
tools.description: List the top-level sheets, reports, dashboards, and folders in a workspace.

tools.name: browse_folder
tools.description: List the direct children of a folder (nested folders, sheets, reports, dashboards).

tools.name: list_workspaces
tools.description: List all accessible Smartsheet workspaces with pagination.

tools.name: get_sheet_summary
tools.description: Retrieve sheet data with optional filtering, sorting, and column selection.

tools.name: create_sheet_in_workspace
tools.description: Create a new blank sheet with custom columns in a workspace.

tools.name: create_sheet_in_folder
tools.description: Create a new blank sheet with custom columns in a folder.

tools.name: get_sheet_version
tools.description: Get the current sheet version number for lightweight change detection.

tools.name: create_sheet_in_workspace_from_template
tools.description: Clone a template sheet into a workspace.

tools.name: create_sheet_in_folder_from_template
tools.description: Clone a template sheet into a folder.

tools.name: get_columns
tools.description: Get column definitions (names, types, options) for a sheet.

tools.name: add_columns
tools.description: Add one or more columns to a sheet.

tools.name: update_column
tools.description: Update a column's properties (title, type, options, format).

tools.name: delete_column
tools.description: Delete a column from a sheet.

tools.name: add_rows
tools.description: Add one or more rows to a sheet with cell values.

tools.name: update_rows
tools.description: Update cell values on existing rows.

tools.name: delete_rows
tools.description: Delete rows from a sheet.

tools.name: get_cell_history
tools.description: Get the change history for a specific cell.

tools.name: list_attachments
tools.description: List all attachments on a sheet.

tools.name: list_row_attachments
tools.description: List attachments on a specific row.

tools.name: list_discussion_attachments
tools.description: List attachments on a discussion.

tools.name: get_attachment
tools.description: Get attachment metadata and download URL.

tools.name: attach_url_to_sheet
tools.description: Attach a URL to a sheet.

tools.name: attach_url_to_row
tools.description: Attach a URL to a row.

tools.name: attach_url_to_comment
tools.description: Attach a URL to a comment.

tools.name: delete_attachment
tools.description: Delete an attachment.

tools.name: list_sheet_discussions
tools.description: List all discussions on a sheet.

tools.name: list_row_discussions
tools.description: List discussions on a specific row.

tools.name: get_discussion
tools.description: Get a discussion with its comments.

tools.name: create_discussion_on_row
tools.description: Start a new discussion on a row.

tools.name: create_discussion_on_sheet
tools.description: Start a new discussion on a sheet.

tools.name: delete_discussion
tools.description: Delete a discussion.

tools.name: add_comment
tools.description: Add a comment to an existing discussion.

tools.name: update_comment
tools.description: Update an existing comment.

tools.name: delete_comment
tools.description: Delete a comment.

tools.name: list_reports
tools.description: List accessible Smartsheet reports.

tools.name: get_report
tools.description: Retrieve a report's contents.

tools.name: list_dashboards
tools.description: List accessible Smartsheet dashboards.

tools.name: get_dashboard
tools.description: Retrieve a dashboard's contents.

tools.name: create_workspace
tools.description: Create a new top-level workspace.

tools.name: create_folder_in_workspace
tools.description: Create a folder inside a workspace.

tools.name: create_folder_in_folder
tools.description: Create a nested folder inside another folder.

connectors.name: Apple Shortcuts
connectors.description: Run shortcuts from your macOS Shortcuts library. 'Trigger my Morning Briefing shortcut' or 'List my workflow shortcuts'.

tools.name: apple_shortcuts_list
tools.description: List all available shortcuts, optionally filtered by folder.

tools.name: apple_shortcuts_run
tools.description: Run a named shortcut with optional text input.

connectors.name: OpusClip
connectors.description: OpusClip MCP server. Turn long-form videos into short clips, manage clip projects and collections, run profanity censor jobs, and schedule platform-specific social posts. 'Clip this YouTube talk into vertical shorts' or 'Schedule the top three clips to LinkedIn for Tuesday morning'.

tools.name: configure_opus_api_key
tools.description: Configure the OpusClip API key for this session. WHEN TO USE: only call this if you get an error saying "Opus API key not configured" or "AUTH_FAILED". HOW USER GETS THEIR KEY: go to https://app.opus.pro/settings/integration-tokens and create a new token. After configuring, all other opus_* tools will work immediately.

tools.name: opus_get_brand_templates
tools.description: List the OpusClip brand templates available to your organisation. Brand templates control captions, fonts, colours, intro/outro cards, and other styling. Use a brand_template_id from this response with opus_create_project or opus_upload_video. NOTE: The Opus API requires the `q` parameter to equal "mine" — calling the endpoint without it returns an empty body. The schema below makes the only legal value compile-time enforced.

tools.name: opus_create_project
tools.description: Create a new OpusClip clipping project from a publicly importable long-form video URL. After this returns a projectId, poll opus_get_project to track stage progression (PENDING → QUEUED → IMPORT → CURATE → REFINE → RENDER → UPLOAD → COMPLETE). When complete, call opus_get_clips to retrieve the generated clips. For local files, use opus_upload_video instead — it handles upload-link generation, GCS resumable upload, and project creation in a single tool call.

tools.name: opus_get_project
tools.description: Retrieve the current status and metadata of an OpusClip project. Check the `stage` field — when it reaches "COMPLETE" the clips are ready (use opus_get_clips). If `stage` is "STALLED" or `error` is set, the project has failed.

tools.name: opus_get_clips
tools.description: List the exportable clips for a given project or collection. Each clip has a full-clip id of the form `{projectId}.{curationId}`, plus preview/export URLs (`uriForPreview`, `uriForExport`) on Google Cloud Storage. Choose `q: "findByProjectId"` and pass `projectId`, OR `q: "findByCollectionId"` and pass `collectionId`. Pagination via `pageNum` (starts at 1) and `pageSize`.

tools.name: opus_share_project
tools.description: Update the sharing visibility of an OpusClip project. `DEFAULT`: team members can open / edit / export. `PUBLIC`: anyone with the link can open / edit / export.

tools.name: opus_upload_video
tools.description: Upload a local video file to OpusClip and create a clipping project in a single step. Orchestrates the 4-step Opus / Google Cloud Storage resumable upload: (1) request an upload link, (2) start a GCS resumable session, (3) PUT the video bytes (with automatic offset-query recovery on ambiguous failures), (4) create the project. Pass `file_path` as an absolute filesystem path. Optional `brandTemplateId`, `curationPref`, `renderPref`, `importPref`, `uploadedVideoAttr`, and `conclusionActions` are forwarded to `opus_create_project`. On retryable network failures the same `uploadId` is reused, so calling this tool twice in a row will NOT create two billable projects. Per-chunk upload timeout is OPUS_UPLOAD_TIMEOUT_MS (default 600s).

tools.name: opus_create_censor_job
tools.description: Create a censor job that processes a specific clip and bleeps / mutes profanity. Returns a jobId. Poll opus_get_censor_job_status until status is "CONCLUDED" (success) or "FAILED". Set `options.beepSound: true` to substitute a beep for censored words; default is to mute them.

tools.name: opus_get_censor_job_status
tools.description: Poll the status of an OpusClip censor job. Status values: "QUEUED" / "PROCESSING" (still running, poll again), "CONCLUDED" (success), "FAILED" (check error), "UNKNOWN" (Opus could not determine the state — surfaced as UPSTREAM_STATUS_UNKNOWN). The response includes `next_poll_after_seconds` honouring Retry-After when present.

tools.name: opus_create_collection
tools.description: Create a new clip collection to organise your clips. Returns the new collectionId. Use opus_add_clip_to_collection to populate it.

tools.name: opus_get_collections
tools.description: List your OpusClip collections. Use `q: "mine"` to list all collections on your account, or `q: "findByContentId"` (with `contentId` set to a full clip id like "{projectId}.{curationId}") to find which collections contain a specific clip.

tools.name: opus_export_collection
tools.description: Export all clips from a collection. Returns a `contentList` of `{ contentId, uriForExport }` pairs — the uriForExport URLs are Google Cloud Storage download links for the clip MP4s.

tools.name: opus_delete_collection
tools.description: Delete a clip collection. IMPORTANT: only the collection container is deleted — the clips themselves are preserved and still accessible via opus_get_clips with `q: "findByProjectId"`.

tools.name: opus_add_clip_to_collection
tools.description: Add a clip to an existing collection. `contentId` is the full clip ID in the form `{projectId}.{curationId}` (e.g. "Q20107035eDq.CU0b92e6") — get it from opus_get_clips.

tools.name: opus_remove_clip_from_collection
tools.description: Remove a clip from a collection. IMPORTANT: Opus exposes this operation as a POST to /api/collection-contents/delete-collection-contents (NOT a DELETE). The clip itself is not deleted — only the collection membership is removed.

tools.name: opus_get_social_accounts
tools.description: List the social accounts connected to your Opus organisation. Returns `postAccountId`, `subAccountId` (where applicable), `platform`, and the platform-side `extUser*` identifiers. Use these IDs with opus_publish_post, opus_schedule_post, and opus_create_social_copy_job.

tools.name: opus_create_social_copy_job
tools.description: Create an asynchronous social-copy generation job. Opus produces a platform-specific title, description, and hashtags for a clip + destination account. Returns a jobId — poll opus_get_social_copy_job until status is "COMPLETED" or "FAILED".

tools.name: opus_get_social_copy_job
tools.description: Poll the status and result of a social-copy generation job. Status values: "RUNNING" (still generating), "COMPLETED" (returns title/description/hashtags), "FAILED". Includes `next_poll_after_seconds` honouring Retry-After.

tools.name: opus_publish_post
tools.description: Publish a clip immediately to a connected social account. For X (formerly Twitter) each post costs 1 credit. Returns a `postId`. The clip is queued for upload by Opus to the destination platform.

tools.name: opus_schedule_post
tools.description: Schedule a clip for future publishing. `publishAt` must be a future UTC ISO 8601 timestamp (e.g. "2026-06-01T16:00:00.000Z"). For X (formerly Twitter) each post costs 1 credit. Returns a `scheduleId` you can pass to opus_cancel_scheduled_post.

tools.name: opus_cancel_scheduled_post
tools.description: Cancel a scheduled social post BEFORE its publishAt time. After publishAt the post is irrevocably handed to the platform — cancellation is no longer possible.
